The live Chapter 20 blockers are Theorem 20.3's stored-Householder QR theorem without extra source-side invariants, Theorem 20.7's weighted-LS row-wise QR dependency, the remaining Theorem 20.8 Eldén--Cox--Higham transfer, and the fully rounded Theorem 20.10 finite-precision GQR path if that stronger computed route is selected. Theorem 20.1's Wedin perturbation transfer is closed at the repository residual-definition/column-orthogonality API by the solution, residual, and combined wrappers listed below; stricter source-minimal API cleanup is optional and is not a live blocker. Theorem 20.5, Theorem 20.9, (20.18)-(20.19), the current Theorem 20.4 surface, selected exact Problems 20.10 and 20.11, and the recorded Theorem 20.8 algebraic handoff layers are closed at their listed APIs. For the current Theorem 20.8 Gram-pseudoinverse branch, source full row rank of `B` now discharges `det(rectGram B) != 0`, but `theorem20_8_gram_AP_rectGram_det_zero_counterexample` shows that the source (20.24) rank assumptions do not imply `det(rectGram(AP)) != 0` for the concrete Gram instantiation. The determinant-facing `(AP)^+` shortcut is therefore not source-derivable in general; the remaining viable Gram-branch obligations are source construction or instantiation of a rank-tolerant `(AP)^+`, the reduced `AP*(y-x)` equation, and the unconditional reduced-LS/Wedin residual-gap estimate. The same-residual norm-dominated subcase and the conditional residual-gap source-radius packaging now expose source-shaped first-order plus `eps^2` forms, but they do not prove the missing residual-gap estimate or close the full Eldén--Cox--Higham theorem. The matrix form of (20.24), `(AP)^+ AP = P`, is now proved from the reduced nullspace left-inverse and exposed for the source stacked-full-column-rank MP-Gram branch, while keeping the chosen `(AP)^+` Moore--Penrose certificate explicit. The rank-tolerant route also has a generic adapter from `RectMoorePenrosePseudoinverse AP APplus` plus `B*(AP)^T = 0` to the needed `B*(AP)^+ = 0` and nullspace left-inverse handoff; the source-full-row-rank Gram-`B` wrappers discharge the `B^+ = B^T(BB^T)^{-1}` right-inverse and transpose-range proof. The latest bridges prove the converse projector-range handoff `B*(AP)^+ = 0 -> P*(AP)^+ = (AP)^+`, package `RectMoorePenrosePseudoinverse AP APplus` plus `B*AP^T = 0` directly as that projector-range identity, and derive the source matrix identity `(AP)^+AP=P` from the same MP/transpose-range/null-intersection route, so later rank-tolerant `(AP)^+` instantiations can use either source nullspace, transpose-range, projector-range, or equation-(20.24) matrix form without strengthening to a determinant inverse. Deriving computed QR nonbreakdown from only full rank plus explicit smallness/no-underflow assumptions remains optional strengthening if the explicit domain assumption is not accepted. End-of-chapter Problems/exercises are selectable optional targets; Problem 20.10 is covered by `IsLSEMinimizer.of_lagrange_normal_equations`, `IsLSEMinimizer.exists_lagrange_normal_equations_of_fullRowRank`, and `isLSEMinimizer_iff_exists_lagrange_normal_equations_of_fullRowRank` under the source full-row-rank constraint qualification; Problem 20.11 is covered by the unconstrained coefficient reduction listed below.

- Current Theorem 20.7 row-scale route-audit increment: `theorem20_7_initialRowMax_sorted_not_imp_sqrt_row_domination_two_by_one` proves that source row sorting by `max_j |a_ij|` does not by itself imply the stronger `sqrt(m)` row-scale domination hypothesis used by the Chapter 19 accumulated-error transfer.  The concrete two-row, one-column counterexample has sorted row scales `2, 1`, but the pivot scale `2` is not bounded by `sqrt(2) * 1`.  This rules out discharging `hArow0` merely from source row sorting; a real QR source-initial route must either prove the domination from an additional source/algorithm invariant or keep it as an explicit side condition.  Validation at `4d25f3f0`: focused `lake env lean LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/LeastSquares/LSE.lean`, target `lake build LeanFpAnalysis.FP.Algorithms.LeastSquares.LSE`, `git diff --check`, touched-file placeholder scan, and focused namespaced `#print axioms` passed; the axiom check reported only `propext`, `Classical.choice`, and `Quot.sound`, and the verified head was pushed to both `end-to-end-rebuild` and `main`.

`theorem20_10_householder_constructed_gqr_reversed_rhs_tail_partB_xhat_minimizer_of_frobenius_rank_margins_composed_conservative_gamma` instantiates the perturbation operator-norm side conditions from the concrete Householder Frobenius bounds, and `theorem20_10_householder_constructed_gqr_reversed_rhs_tail_partB_xhat_minimizer_of_source_ranks_frobenius_margins_composed_conservative_gamma` derives the lower-bound predicates from `LSEFullRowRank B` and `LSEStackedFullColumnRank A B`.  The remaining full computed-GQR work is proving or justifying strict smallness against the induced noncomputable source margins if required, plus sharpening RHS/composite coefficients if required.
- Latest Theorem 20.10 source-rank radius note: `theorem20_10_householder_sourceRankBudget`, `theorem20_10_householder_sourceRankBudget_nonneg`, `theorem20_10_householder_sourceRankRadius`, and `theorem20_10_householder_sourceRankRadius_pos` package the source-rank branch's smallness surface as one canonical comparison against the minimum of the `B^T` and stacked `[A; B]` margins.  `theorem20_10_householder_sourceRankBudget_lt_sourceRankRadius_of_half_bounds` splits the single rank-radius smallness condition into two half-radius component budgets, `theorem20_10_householder_sourceRankBudget_lt_sourceRankRadius_of_max_gamma_sum_bound` gives the compact sufficient condition `max(gammaA,gammaB) * (||A||_F + ||B||_F) < sourceRankRadius`, and `theorem20_10_householder_constructed_gqr_reversed_rhs_tail_partB_xhat_minimizer_of_source_ranks_rank_radius_composed_conservative_gamma` consumes the single strict condition `sourceRankBudget < sourceRankRadius` and derives the two inequalities required by the prior source-rank Frobenius-margin theorem.  `theorem20_10_householder_constructed_gqr_reversed_rhs_tail_partB_xhat_minimizer_of_source_ranks_max_gamma_sum_bound_composed_conservative_gamma` exposes the compact max-gamma condition directly on the returned-vector Part B surface.  `theorem20_10_householder_sourceRankGammaThreshold`, `theorem20_10_householder_sourceRankGammaThreshold_pos`, `theorem20_10_householder_max_gamma_sum_bound_of_max_gamma_lt_sourceRankGammaThreshold`, and `theorem20_10_householder_constructed_gqr_reversed_rhs_tail_partB_xhat_minimizer_of_source_ranks_gamma_threshold_composed_conservative_gamma` now package a positive source-rank gamma threshold and use it directly on the returned-vector theorem.  This tightens the caller-facing rank-preservation surface and records a nonvacuous smallness threshold; it still does not prove that a concrete FP model's gamma coefficients satisfy the threshold or sharpen the conservative RHS coefficient.

## Bottleneck and Audit Ledger

Entries marked red/partial are active because repeated proof and audit passes have produced useful conditional infrastructure while leaving the same selected-scope foundation exposed.  Closed entries are retained only as regression notes.  Progress on active rows should be counted only when one of the listed next dependency theorems is proved, a listed route is ruled out with Lean/source evidence, or the theorem surface is corrected to match the source.  Do not add adjacent wrappers for active rows unless the wrapper closes one of the named dependencies.

| Source claim | Exact blocking Lean target | Dependency list | Local candidates and failed/insufficient routes | Chosen route/status | Next concrete theorem | Validation command |

The WKS row remains active only for the full `theta = infinity` matrix-only limiting formula/equality.

Post-`17e69c76` update for the Theorem 20.10 row: `theorem20_10_householder_constructed_gqr_reversed_rhs_tail_partA_mixed_stability_of_diagonal_composed_conservative_gamma` unwraps the constructed reversed-RHS original-source Part A certificate to the mixed-stability core.  This adds the `DeltaX` relation for the computed transformed-tail `xhat` to an exact minimizer of the perturbed source problem under the named conservative composite coefficients; at that stage, the remaining computed-GQR blockers were perturbed rank preservation from source hypotheses, the later returned-vector step, and optional coefficient sharpening to the printed RHS surface.

Post-`8dba5c05` update for the Theorem 20.10 row: `theorem20_10_householder_constructed_gqr_reversed_rhs_tail_partB_backward_error_of_diagonal_composed_conservative_gamma` unwraps the constructed reversed-RHS Part B certificate to the exact Part B backward-error core under the named conservative composite coefficients.  This closed the certificate-to-core handoff for the constructed rounded `B^T`/reversed-`A Q2` route; at that stage the remaining computed-GQR blockers still included returned-vector identification, which is now superseded by the post-`e81c9d31` theorem.

Post-`d95b7fb0` update for the Theorem 20.7 row: `theorem20_7_stageRowMax_le_of_entry_le_nat`, `theorem20_7_stageBMax_le_of_entry_le_nat`, and `theorem20_7_alphaBetaMax_le_of_uniform_entry_growth_nat` convert Nat-indexed stage bounds with side condition `k < n` into the finite rowwise-ratio surface already used for Theorem 20.7.  This removes a Nat/Fin impedance mismatch with the Chapter 19 row-wise QR dependency wrappers; it does not prove the full Powell--Reid/Cox--Higham row-wise QR stability theorem or the displayed perturbation bounds themselves.

Post-`a3a62be7` update for the Theorem 20.7 row: `theorem20_7_initialWeightedRowMax_nonneg` proves the nonnegativity side condition for the weighted row denominator, `theorem20_7_alphaBetaMax_le_of_uniform_geometric_entry_growth_nat` turns stagewise `G^k` row and right-hand-side bounds into the fixed-horizon `G^(n-1)` ratio bound, and `theorem20_7_alphaBetaMax_le_of_active_row_geometric_entry_growth_nat` specializes that handoff to `H19.Theorem19_6.active_row_growth_factor`.  This is the scalar/geometric bridge needed after Chapter 19 supplies the actual row-wise QR stage bounds; it still does not close the full Theorem 20.7 perturbation theorem.

Post-`df38a3d9` update for the Theorem 20.7 row: `theorem20_7_alpha_le_alphaBetaMax`, `theorem20_7_beta_le_alphaBetaMax`, `theorem20_7_alpha_le_of_alphaBetaMax_le`, `theorem20_7_beta_le_of_alphaBetaMax_le`, `theorem20_7_stageRowMax_le_mul_initial_of_alpha_le`, `theorem20_7_stageWeightedRowMax_le_mul_initialWeighted_of_beta_le`, `theorem20_7_stageRowMax_le_mul_initial_of_alphaBetaMax_le`, `theorem20_7_stageWeightedRowMax_le_mul_initialWeighted_of_alphaBetaMax_le`, `theorem20_7_stageAEntry_le_mul_initial_of_alphaBetaMax_le_nat`, and `theorem20_7_stageBEntry_le_mul_initialWeighted_of_alphaBetaMax_le_nat` add the reverse direction from a proven finite `max_i {alpha_i, beta_i}` bound to per-row ratio, staged maximum, and Nat-indexed staged-entry bounds.  This makes the ratio surface usable by downstream perturbation-budget assembly, but the full Theorem 20.7 row remains open until the QR rowwise stability dependency and final weighted-LS perturbation certificate are proved.

Post-`84a10123` update for the Theorem 20.7 row: `theorem20_7_alphaBetaMax_le_of_row_sorting_geometric_entry_growth_nat` adds a source-shaped bridge for the displayed row-sorting constant.  If Ch19 supplies staged row and right-hand-side bounds with the one-time `sqrt(m)` factor and per-step `H19.Theorem19_6.rowwise_step_growth_factor = 1 + sqrt 2`, the finite `max_i {alpha_i, beta_i}` coefficient is bounded by `sqrt(m) * (1 + sqrt 2)^(n-1)`.  This bridge removes the need to route the printed sentence through the more conservative `active_row_growth_factor^k` adapter, but it remains conditional on the missing QR rowwise stability theorem.

Post-`e81c9d31` update for the Theorem 20.10 row: `theorem20_10_householder_constructed_gqr_reversed_rhs_tail_partB_xhat_minimizer_of_diagonal_composed_conservative_gamma` preserves the constructed returned-vector fact through the original-source perturbation composition.  Under the same constructed `S`/`L22` nonzero-diagonal branch, it returns source-shaped `DeltaA`, `DeltaB`, `Deltab`, and `Deltad = 0`, proves the Part B bounds with the named conservative composite coefficients, and proves that the named transformed-tail `xhat` is the exact minimizer of the displayed perturbed LSE problem.  Validation: focused `lake env lean LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/LeastSquares/LSE.lean`, `git diff --check`, touched-file placeholder scan, merge with latest `origin/main`, full `lake build`, and push to both `end-to-end-rebuild` and `main` at `38a7d4dd` passed. Post-`fdbd334e` update for the Theorem 20.10 row: `theorem20_10_householder_constructed_gqr_reversed_rhs_tail_partB_certificate_of_diagonal_composed_conservative_gamma` promotes the constructed reversed-RHS original-source Part A certificate to a Part B certificate under the named conservative composite coefficients. Post-`c605ef76` update for the (20.20)-(20.21) WKS row: `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorEtaF_eq_formulaRHS_of_formulaMatrixRowRank_eq_card_of_formulaMatrixSigmaMin_attainer_projected_eq_zero_of_dot_b_ne_zero` and `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorEtaF_eq_formulaRHS_of_left_panel_rowRank_eq_card_of_formulaMatrixSigmaMin_attainer_projected_eq_zero_of_dot_b_ne_zero` remove the explicit printed outer-minimum branch-selection hypothesis from the degenerate source-block attainer route.  Under full source-block row rank, the theorem handles the zero-RHS branch, the `phi` branch, and the row-side `sigma_min` branch internally; the left-panel wrapper supplies source-block row rank from full row rank of the original data matrix.  This is real progress on the WKS packaging surface, but it still assumes every exact source-block `sigma_min` attainer has zero residual-projector component and nonzero source dot product, so the genuine nondegenerate WKS construction, eigenvalue branch formula, and `theta = infinity` limiting case remain open.

Post-`00a2404d`/`7e5e03ca` update for the Theorem 20.3 row: `theorem20_3_householder_qr_ls_backward_error_compactBudget_of_activePivot_rowMax_dualBudget_actualUnitRoundoff` packages the actual-unit-roundoff active-pivot row-max/dual-budget exact-minimizer route under a source-facing Theorem 20.3 name.  It gives the same compact-sequence Frobenius/RHS exact perturbed least-squares conclusion as the stored-lower compact wrapper, but it no longer exposes local diagonal dominance or the `Dcap`/`Ncap` finite-max smallness inequality.  The tightened wrapper consumes the finite scalar stage-budget/row-max comparison defect and derives the pointwise comparison internally.  The remaining visible assumptions are determinant/conditioning data for leading blocks, dual compact-budget data, row-max diagonal defect, scalar comparison defect, active-pivot selection, stored-loop recurrence, signed `alpha`, and compact global-product smallness.

Post-`3314126f`/`cc9d4e9c` update for the Theorem 20.3 row: `theorem20_3_householder_qr_ls_backward_error_compactBudget_of_activePivot_stageDiag_dualBudget_actualUnitRoundoff` packages the actual-unit-roundoff active-pivot stage-diagonal/dual-budget exact-minimizer route under a source-facing Theorem 20.3 name. This wrapper is still conditional and has now been sharpened by the post-`5a5d5abd` horizon-budget surface below.

Post-`287096a2` update for the Theorem 20.3 row: `theorem20_3_householder_qr_ls_backward_error_compactBudget_of_activePivot_stageDiag_dualBudget_horizonBudget_actualUnitRoundoff` routes the stage-diagonal branch through `StoredQRDisplayedRowBudgetControl.of_signed_stage_uniformBudget_globalCompactBudget_activeMaxPivot_kappaInf_dualBudget_stageDiagDefect_horizonBudget`.  It proves the same compact-sequence Frobenius/RHS exact perturbed least-squares conclusion while deriving stage-budget nonnegativity on the QR horizon, a clamped monotone horizon budget, and the displayed row-budget certificate internally from the finite global compact recurrence, the full initial-block bound, and `0 <= stageBudget 0`.  Compared with the previous stage-diagonal wrapper, it removes the separate per-entry displayed-upper initial hypothesis, the caller-facing global stage-budget monotonicity hypothesis, and the all-stage nonnegativity hypothesis.  It remains conditional on determinant/conditioning data, dual compact-budget data, active-pivot selection, stored-loop recurrence, signed `alpha`, the finite stage-diagonal defect, the full initial-block bound, initial budget nonnegativity, and compact global-product smallness.

Post-`7157164e` update for the Theorem 20.10 row: `theorem20_10_partB_certificate_of_householder_components_conservative_gamma` promotes the verified concrete Householder perturbation components to the Part B certificate boundary.  It reuses the existing `DeltaA`, `DeltaB`, `Deltab`, and `Deltad` witnesses and proves the Part B source-shaped norm bounds with the conservative RHS coefficient, while requiring only the genuine perturbed full-row-rank and stacked-full-column-rank hypotheses to construct `Theorem20_10PartBPerturbationCertificate`.  This closes the component-bound packaging dependency for Part B; it deliberately does not prove perturbed rank preservation, instantiate the complete rounded GQR factorization, identify the final computed vector with the perturbed exact minimizer, or sharpen the RHS coefficient to Higham's printed `gamma_tilde_mn ||b||_2` bound.

Post-`cc6adea1` update for the Theorem 20.10 row: `theorem20_10_partB_certificate_of_partA_certificate` turns any `Theorem20_10PartAPerturbationCertificate` into a `Theorem20_10PartBPerturbationCertificate` under `0 <= gammaB`.  It constructs `Deltad` as `DeltaB*xhat`, reuses the Part A perturbed rank and `DeltaA`/`DeltaB`/`Deltab` bounds, and weakens the Part A `Deltab` bound by adding the nonnegative Part B term `gammaB * ||A||_F * ||xhat||_2`.  This closes the generic Part B certificate handoff for all already-certified Part A routes; it still does not identify the fully rounded computed vector with a perturbed exact minimizer or sharpen the conservative RHS coefficient.

Post-`3abe803e` update for the Theorem 20.10 row: `theorem20_10_partB_certificate_of_nonempty_partA_certificate` exposes the Part A-to-Part B conversion for constructed-source routes that return `Nonempty` certificates, and `theorem20_10_partB_certificate_of_constructed_source_householder_rhs_conservative_bound` applies it to the rounded-Householder-RHS Part A certificate.  This gives a Part B certificate for the rounded transformed-tail path under the same conservative RHS coefficient and supplied-GQR/Householder-panel hypotheses as the Part A route; it does not instantiate the full rounded `B^T`/`A Q2` GQR factorization or identify the final fully computed vector.

Post-`d82b432e` update for the Theorem 20.9 row: the full associated-row tall (20.28) route is now available conditionally through `finAddAssocEquiv`, `GramSchmidtOrthonormalColumns.reindexRowsEquiv`, `IsOrthogonal.reindexRowsColsEquiv`, `GQRAQTallAssocCase.exists_of_qr_reversed_cols`, `GQRAQTallAssocCase.exists_of_mgs_reversed_cols`, `GeneralizedQRFactorization.exists_of_tall_mgs_constraint_and_full_mgs_assoc_shape`, and `GeneralizedQRFactorization.exists_of_tall_fullRowRank_constraint_and_full_mgs_assoc_shape`.  This closes the exact row-association and full-`AQ` exact-MGS construction surface, but it intentionally leaves the remaining source-faithful gap visible: the book's rank assumptions do not by themselves justify nonbreakdown of MGS on the whole transformed `AQ` block when that block may be rank-deficient.  The next dependency is therefore a rank-tolerant associated (20.28) construction, or a proof that a narrower source-selected theorem is allowed to expose the full-`AQ` MGS nonbreakdown hypothesis.

Post-`62b56855` update for the Theorem 20.10 row: `theorem20_10_householder_concrete_perturbation_components_bound` now combines the verified Householder component perturbations into a single tuple.  It returns `DeltaA`, `DeltaB`, `Deltab`, and `Deltad`, with the trailing `A Q2` product identity, the `B^T` QR perturbation identity, the transformed-RHS identity, the constraint-action identity, and the component norm bounds.  This closes a downstream packaging dependency only; the printed `Deltab` coefficient, remaining triangular-solve perturbations, perturbed rank preservation, and computed-`xhat` identification remain open.

Post-`0b5ac2e7` update for the Theorem 20.10 row: `theorem20_10_gqr_forwardSub_triangular_solve_perturbation_bound` closes the first concrete triangular-solve perturbation dependency for the GQR method.  Given constructed exact GQR data with nonzero diagonals in the lower-triangular `S` and `L22` blocks, it applies `forwardSub_backward_error` to the actual computed `fl_forwardSub fp p S d` and `fl_forwardSub fp q L22 (U^T b - L21*y1hat)` calls and returns componentwise-bounded `DeltaS` and `DeltaL22` witnesses satisfying the exact perturbed triangular equations.  It does not yet transport those factor perturbations into a final `DeltaX` bound, prove perturbed rank preservation, or identify the final computed `xhat`.

Post-`333c5013` update for the Theorem 20.10 row: `theorem20_10_gqr_forwardSub_triangular_solve_frob_perturbation_bound` upgrades the GQR triangular-solve witnesses to source-shaped Frobenius bounds.  It reuses the componentwise `DeltaS` and `DeltaL22` witnesses from the actual `fl_forwardSub` calls and applies `frobNorm_le_const_mul_frobNorm_of_entrywise_abs_le`, yielding `||DeltaS||_F <= gamma_p ||S||_F` and `||DeltaL22||_F <= gamma_q ||L22||_F` while preserving the exact perturbed triangular equations.  It still does not transport these factor perturbations into a final `DeltaX` bound, prove perturbed rank preservation, or identify the final computed `xhat`.

Post-`32810724` update for the Theorem 20.10 row: the supplied-factor computed GQR path is now named by `theorem20_10_gqr_y1hat`, `theorem20_10_gqr_rhs2hat`, `theorem20_10_gqr_y2hat`, and `theorem20_10_gqr_xhat`.  The wrapper `theorem20_10_gqr_xhat_triangular_solve_frob_perturbation_bound` proves that this named `xhat = Q [y1hat; y2hat]` carries the same componentwise and Frobenius-bounded `DeltaS`/`DeltaL22` perturbation witnesses for the actual triangular solves.  This closes the bookkeeping part of computed-`xhat` identification for supplied GQR data, but it still does not prove exact-minimizer status for the final computed vector, the final `DeltaX` relation, or perturbed source-rank preservation.

Post-`4aa618db` update for the Theorem 20.9 row: the previous full-`AQ` MGS nonbreakdown gap is superseded for the tall associated-row route by exact Householder QR. `exactHouseholderQRPanel_R`, `exactHouseholderQRPanel_Q`, `exactHouseholderQR_R`, `exactHouseholderQR_Q`, and their orthogonality/upper-trapezoidal/zero-backward-error lemmas provide a rank-tolerant exact QR panel. `GQRAQTallCase.exists_of_exact_householder_reversed_cols` extracts the thin triangular part needed by the generic tall case, `GQRAQTallAssocCase.exists_of_exact_householder_reversed_cols` transports it to Higham's associated row shape, and `GeneralizedQRFactorization.exists_of_tall_fullRowRank_constraint_and_exact_householder_assoc_shape` plus `GeneralizedQRFactorization.exists_unique_method_solution_of_tall_fullRowRank_constraint_and_exact_householder_assoc_shape` compose it with the `B^T` route. This closes the rank-tolerant tall full-associated (20.28) construction without assuming exact-MGS nonbreakdown for rank-deficient `AQ`. Remaining Theorem 20.9 rows are numeric rank equivalence, computed GQR stability if selected, and any wide associated rank-tolerant analogue if required.

Post-`199d2826` update for the Theorem 20.9 row: the wide associated-column route now has the same rank-tolerant exact-Householder treatment. `GQRAQWideAssocCase.exists_of_trailing_exact_householder_reversed_cols` applies square exact Householder QR to the column-reversed trailing block and feeds the existing wide associated-shape constructor; `GeneralizedQRFactorization.exists_of_wide_fullRowRank_constraint_and_exact_householder_assoc_shape` and `GeneralizedQRFactorization.exists_unique_method_solution_of_wide_fullRowRank_constraint_and_exact_householder_assoc_shape` compose it with the `B^T` route and exact method package. This closes the rank-tolerant wide full-associated (20.28) construction without assuming exact-MGS nonbreakdown for the trailing block.

Post-`d8da13c5` update for the Theorem 20.9 row: the exact source rank/nonsingularity sentence after (20.28) is now attached to the constructed exact-Householder tall and wide routes. `GeneralizedQRFactorization.exists_unique_method_solution_with_s_l22_diag_ne_zero_of_tall_fullRowRank_constraint_and_exact_householder_assoc_shape` and `GeneralizedQRFactorization.exists_unique_method_solution_with_s_l22_diag_ne_zero_of_wide_fullRowRank_constraint_and_exact_householder_assoc_shape` return constructed GQR data, nonzero diagonals for the triangular `S` and `L22` blocks, unique exact triangular solve coordinates, and the unique exact LSE minimizer under the source full-row-rank and stacked-full-column-rank assumptions. This closes the exact Theorem 20.9 rank/nonsingularity row; finite-precision GQR stability is Theorem 20.10.

Post-`fdd536c0` update for the (20.18)-(20.19) row: the prior source-rank bridge gap is superseded by `lsRealRectColRank_rectMatMulVec_eq_zero_of_colRank_eq_card`, `lsRealRectColRank_rectMatMulVec_injective_of_colRank_eq_card`, and `exists_lsScaledAugmentedMatrix_kappa2_bounds_of_rightGram_basis_of_colRank_eq_card`. The finite ordered-extrema alignment is now also available through `lsScaledAugmentedBranchSigmaMin_rectRightGramBasis_eq_rectSingularValue` and `lsScaledAugmentedBranchSigmaMax_rectRightGramBasis_eq_rectSingularValue`; at this point the row still needed final source-facing packaging over those ordered extrema.

The right-Gram route now gives constructed `u`, `v`, and `w` branch data, full-column-rank input, and balanced `kappa2` bounds with the scaling and ratio written using ordered real right-Gram singular values `rectSingularValue A`. Proof-source update: `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorFormulaRHS_le_costF_of_feasible_finite_theta` and `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorFormulaRHS_le_etaF_of_finite_theta` now combine the `p = 0` exact-residual branch with the nonzero-expanded-residual branch and transfer the all-feasible pointwise lower bound to the infimum model `eta_F`. The source-block `sigma_min` constructive upper-bound branch, eigenvalue branch formula, and `theta = infinity` limiting case remain open.  No GPT Pro oracle consultation was used for this update.

Proof-source update: `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorEtaF_eq_formulaRHS_and_pos_of_phi_le_sigmaMin` specializes the positive upper-certificate handoff to the printed scalar branch `phi <= sigma_min [A phi(I-r r^+)]`.  The exact-fit perturbation gives the upper inequality, the finite-positive lower-bound side gives equality and positivity, and the source-block `sigma_min` construction, eigenvalue branch formula, and `theta = infinity` limiting case remain open.  No GPT Pro oracle consultation was used for this update.

Proof-source update: `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorRankOneDeltaA`, `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorRankOneDeltaA_transpose_mul`, `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorRankOneDeltaA_frobNormSq`, `LSNormwiseBackwardErrorFeasible.rankOne_source_residual_witness`, `LSNormwiseBackwardErrorFeasible.exists_rankOne_source_residual_witness`, and `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorEtaF_le_rankOne_source_residual_witness` add the first concrete source-block upper-route construction for the WKS positive branch.  For any nonzero expanded residual `p`, the rank-one `DeltaA` realizes the left action `-A^T p`, has the exact Frobenius norm `||A^T p||_2^2 / ||p||_2^2` in the prescribed-action form, and with the matching `Delta b` makes `y` feasible for the perturbed least-squares problem; the existential package exposes those residual, action, and Frobenius facts, and the new `eta_F` theorem turns the construction into an explicit square-root upper bound.  This is still partial foundation: it does not extract a row-side `sigma_min` singular vector, prove the weighted cost is bounded by the printed RHS, close `eta_F(y) <= RHS` on the `sigma_min` branch, derive the eigenvalue branch formula, or handle `theta = infinity`.  No GPT Pro oracle consultation was used for this update.

Proof-source update: `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorRankOneDeltaA_mulVec` and `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorEtaF_le_rankOne_source_residual_witness_expanded` expose the `DeltaA * y` term in the rank-one WKS witness and rewrite the square-root `eta_F` upper bound with the resulting explicit `Delta b` vector.  This closes the next algebraic dependency needed to compare the rank-one witness cost with a future source-block singular-vector certificate.  It is still partial foundation: it does not choose the row-side `sigma_min` vector, prove the witness cost is bounded by the printed RHS, close the source-block `sigma_min` branch, derive the eigenvalue branch formula, or handle `theta = infinity`.  No GPT Pro oracle consultation was used for this update.

Proof-source update: `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorEtaF_le_formulaRHS_of_rankOne_expanded_cost_le_sigmaMin` packages the source-block `sigma_min` branch around the concrete rank-one witness: once a nonzero expanded residual `p` has the explicit expanded witness cost bounded by the source-block `sigma_min`, and the printed outer minimum selects that branch, the theorem proves `eta_F(y) <=` the right-hand side of (20.21).  This removes the generic upper-inequality handoff for the rank-one route, but it still leaves the genuine source-block singular-vector certificate, eigenvalue branch formula, and `theta = infinity` limiting case open.  No GPT Pro oracle consultation was used for this update.

Proof-source update: `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorEtaF_eq_formulaRHS_and_pos_of_rankOne_expanded_cost_le_sigmaMin` combines the concrete rank-one `sigma_min` upper handoff with the already proved finite-positive WKS lower-bound theorem. Proof-source update: `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorEtaF_le_rankOne_source_residual_witness_scaled` exposes the nonzero scalar freedom in the expanded rank-one WKS witness. This is the next algebraic dependency for optimizing the rank-one witness against a source-block singular-vector certificate; it still does not choose the row-side `sigma_min` vector, prove the expanded witness cost bound, derive the eigenvalue branch formula, or handle `theta = infinity`. Proof-source update: `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorEtaF_le_formulaRHS_of_rankOne_scaled_cost_le_sigmaMin` and `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorEtaF_eq_formulaRHS_and_pos_of_rankOne_scaled_cost_le_sigmaMin` package the scaled rank-one WKS route at the same source-facing surfaces as the unscaled certificate.  Once a nonzero scalar `c` and nonzero expanded residual `p` satisfy the explicit scaled witness cost bound against the source-block `sigma_min`, the first theorem proves `eta_F(y) <= RHS`; under positive finite `theta`, nonzero `y`, non-minimizer, and full source-block row-rank assumptions, the second combines that scaled upper handoff with the already proved lower-bound side to obtain equality and positivity.  The actual singular-vector construction supplying the scaled cost bound, the eigenvalue branch formula, and `theta = infinity` remain open.  No GPT Pro oracle consultation was used for this update.

The generic bridge proves that a certificate `||u||_2^2 + theta^2 ||q||_2^2 ||p||_2^2 <= sigma^2 ||p||_2^2` implies the square-root witness cost bound, and the source-facing theorem applies it with `sigma = sigma_min [A phi(I-r r^+)]` to prove `eta_F(y) <= RHS` when the outer minimum selects that branch.  The singular-vector construction that supplies the squared certificate, the eigenvalue branch formula, and `theta = infinity` remain open.  No GPT Pro oracle consultation was used for this update.

Proof-source update: `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorFormulaMatrix_transpose_vecNorm2Sq_eq` proves the unconditional squared transpose-action expansion for the WKS source block `[A phi(I-r r^+)]`: for arbitrary `p`, the squared norm of the transposed action splits as `||A^T p||_2^2 + phi^2 ||(I-r r^+)p||_2^2`.  This is the algebraic source-block comparison form needed before a genuine row-side `sigma_min` singular-vector certificate can feed the squared rank-one WKS cost bridge.  It still does not choose the minimizing vector, prove the scaled witness cost bound, derive the eigenvalue branch formula, or handle `theta = infinity`. They prove that the free scalar changes only the component parallel to `p`, that every scalar leaves an unavoidable orthogonal component at least `||(I-p p^+)r||_2^2`, and that the explicit optimal scalar realizes exactly that projected residual.  This is progress on the listed optimal-scalar dependency, but it also shows that the current rank-one source-transpose projector-comparison route cannot be closed merely by choosing the scalar; the remaining source-block `sigma_min` upper branch needs a stronger perturbation construction or a different WKS-specific comparison route, plus the eigenvalue branch formula and `theta = infinity`. This rules out the nondegenerate scalar-only projector-comparison route; the next WKS upper-branch work must use a stronger perturbation construction, a degenerate projected-residual subcase, or a different source-specific comparison route. This is still only a subcase: the nondegenerate case is already ruled out for the scalar-only route, and the full WKS source-block `sigma_min` upper branch still needs a stronger perturbation construction or a different source-specific comparison, plus the eigenvalue branch formula and `theta = infinity`. With `r = b - Ay` and `u = A^T p`, the numerator `p^T r + u^T y` is exactly `p^T b`, hence the optimal scale is `(p^T b)/||p||_2^2`.  This makes the nonzero-scale side condition in the degenerate source-transpose handoff auditable against the original right-hand side `b`; it does not prove that side condition, close the nondegenerate route, derive the full source-block construction, prove the eigenvalue branch formula, or handle `theta = infinity`. They show that (20.20) feasibility is exactly the perturbed least-squares normal-equation condition and, equivalently, perturbed residual orthogonality in both the repository residual sign convention and Higham's signed residual convention `r = b - A y`.  This formalizes the local least-squares-consistency step while leaving the WKS spectral lower-bound inequality, constructive upper-bound perturbation, eigenvalue branch formula, and `theta = infinity` limiting case open.  Validation: `lake env lean LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/LeastSquares/LSQRSolve.lean`, `lake build LeanFpAnalysis.FP.Algorithms.LeastSquares.LSQRSolve`, focused `#print axioms` checks, placeholder/conflict/trailing-whitespace scans, `git diff --check`, and full `lake build` passed; axiom checks report only `propext`, `Classical.choice`, and `Quot.sound`, and the full build reported only pre-existing GivensSpec/FastMatMul warnings.
- Latest source-route audit: the WKS red-bottleneck now has an explicit proof-source ledger entry.  The primary formula source is Waldén--Karlson--Sun (1995), DOI `10.1002/nla.1680020308`; direct paper-body inspection was blocked here, so no proof details from that paper are claimed.  The accessible route uses Chang--Golub--Paige--Titley-Peloquin, Sections 3-4, to start with the FLS/NeLS consistency characterization, then splits the remaining positive branch into the lower inequality `formulaRHS <= eta_F(y)` and the upper inequality `eta_F(y) <= formulaRHS`.  The next Lean work is a source-facing consistency-characterization theorem built on `RectLSNormalEquations.iff_isLeastSquaresMinimizer`, not another adjacent WKS wrapper.
- Latest addition: `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorEtaF_le_formulaRHS_of_exists_feasible_cost_le` weakens the WKS upper-bound handoff for (20.21) from an exact attaining-cost witness to a feasible perturbation whose cost is bounded by the printed right-hand side, and `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorEtaF_eq_formulaRHS_and_pos_of_positive_inequality_certificate` repackages the finite-positive-`theta`, full-row-rank positive branch as the two inequality obligations `formulaRHS <= eta_F(y)` and `eta_F(y) <= formulaRHS`.  This corrects the remaining WKS route surface without claiming the spectral lower-bound proof, constructive upper-bound perturbation, eigenvalue branch formula, or `theta = infinity` limiting case.  Validation so far: `lake env lean LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/LeastSquares/LSQRSolve.lean` passed before this ledger update; full milestone validation is recorded in the commit cycle for this row.
- Latest addition: `householderQRRhsPanelGammaClosedGrowthFactor_printedCoeff_lt_factorCoeff` lifts the Theorem 20.4 route-elimination proof to the real RHS coefficient surface.  Under positive unit roundoff and a nonempty panel, the already verified conservative coefficient `2 * householderQRRhsPanelGammaClosedGrowthFactor m p * gamma fp (p * householderConstructApplyGammaIndex m)` is strictly larger than the printed panel coefficient `p * gamma fp (p * householderConstructApplyGammaIndex m)`.  This rules out closing the printed `Delta f` coefficient by a direct comparison of the current conservative coefficient to the source coefficient; the remaining work must sharpen the RHS arithmetic/model before the closed-growth factor appears, or explicitly keep a source-corrected larger coefficient.  Validation: `lake env lean LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/QR/QRSolve.lean` passed before this ledger update; full milestone validation is recorded in the commit cycle for this row.  A GPT Pro oracle consultation was attempted for proof planning but failed before any mathematical prompt was submitted.
- Previous addition: `householderQRRhsPanelGammaClosedGrowthIndex_gt_printedIndex` and `householderQRRhsPanelGammaClosedGrowthIndex_not_le_printedIndex` lift the Theorem 20.4 route-elimination proof from the visible closed-growth factor to the accumulated gamma index itself.  Under nonzero row dimension and nonzero panel step count, the conservative index `householderQRRhsPanelGammaClosedGrowthIndex m p` is strictly larger than the printed radius `p * householderConstructApplyGammaIndex m`, so the current accumulated-index route cannot be identified with Higham's printed `n * gamma` RHS radius.  Validation: `lake env lean LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/QR/QRSolve.lean` passed before this ledger update; full milestone validation is recorded in the commit cycle for this row.
- Previous addition: `householderQRRhsPanelGammaClosedGrowthFactor_gt_panel_steps`, `householderQRRhsPanelGammaClosedGrowthFactor_not_le_panel_steps`, and `two_mul_householderQRRhsPanelGammaClosedGrowthFactor_not_le_panel_steps` formally rule out the Theorem 20.4 factor-compression route for the current concrete Householder RHS recursion.  For every nonempty row dimension `m`, the conservative factor `m^2 * (1 + 2*m^2)^p` is strictly larger than the panel step count `p`, and the visible `2 * factor` coefficient is therefore also not bounded by `p`.  This closes the previously listed `householderQRRhsPanelGammaClosedGrowthFactor_le_n` route as false; the remaining printed-coefficient work must either sharpen the RHS model/arithmetic before this factor appears or record a source-corrected larger coefficient. From a supplied branch-classified orthogonal diagonalization, supplied max/min singular-pair witnesses, `sigmaMin > 0`, `sigmaMin <= sigmaMax`, and `alpha = sigmaMin / sqrt 2`, the new two-sided wrapper proves `sqrt 2 * (sigmaMax / sigmaMin) <= kappa2 C(alpha) Cinv` and `kappa2 C(alpha) Cinv <= 2 * (sigmaMax / sigmaMin)` for the same reciprocal-diagonal inverse candidate.  This gives a compact conditional interval certificate while still leaving open the construction of the complete eigenbasis, proof that the printed branch list exhausts the spectrum with multiplicities, and proof that the supplied singular pairs are globally extremal. Under nonzero `y` and full row rank of the WKS source block (or the original `A` panel), the theorems prove `eta_F(y)` equals the printed RHS by using existing zero-branch closure when the RHS vanishes and requiring lower-bound/attainment certificates only when the printed RHS is positive. This is exact LSE algebra only and does not construct GQR factors or prove computed GQR stability.

This moves Theorem 20.9's supplied-shape route closer to the final source theorem, but still does not construct `Q`, construct `U`, prove the associated (20.28) shape records from actual QR factors, or prove computed GQR stability.

These wrappers handle repeated singular values and the `alpha` left-null eigenspace from supplied component orthogonality, which the distinct-eigenvalue symmetric-matrix orthogonality lemma cannot cover.  This is still complete-basis infrastructure only: it does not prove singular-vector/nullspace basis existence, global multiplicities, or the matrix condition-number formula (20.19).
- Previous addition: `finAppend_sum_mul_eq`, `finAppend_sum_mul_smul_eq`, `lsScaledAugmentedMatrix_singularPair_normalized_dot_eq`, and `lsScaledAugmentedMatrix_singularPair_normalized_dot_eq_zero_of_orthogonal` add the dot-product expansion for source-normalized appended branch vectors in (20.18).  The new source-facing theorem reduces orthogonality of `[u; beta v]` and `[w; gamma z]` to the dot products of the left and right singular-vector components.  This is a complete-orthogonal-basis dependency only: it does not prove existence of a full singular-vector/nullspace basis, global multiplicities, or the matrix condition-number formula (20.19).
- Previous addition: `lsScaledAugmentedEigenvalue_branch_strict_extreme_bounds_of_sigma_bounds` and `lsScaledAugmentedEigenvalue_branch_ratio_lt_extreme_of_strict_sigma_bounds` add strict scalar extremal and branch-ratio envelopes for (20.18)-(20.19).  A singular value strictly inside the source interval has positive branch strictly below the largest branch, negative-branch magnitude strictly above the smallest branch, and branch ratio strictly below the extremal `lambda_+(sigmaMax)/|lambda_-(sigmaMin)|` ratio.  This is still scalar branch infrastructure only: it does not prove the complete eigenbasis, global multiplicities, or the matrix condition-number formula (20.19).
- Previous addition: `lsScaledAugmentedEigenvaluePlus_strictMono_sigma_nonneg` and `lsScaledAugmentedEigenvalueMinus_abs_strictMono_sigma_nonneg` add strict scalar branch monotonicity in the nonnegative singular value for (20.18)-(20.19).  The positive branch and the magnitude of the negative branch are strictly ordered whenever the source singular values are strictly ordered.  This strengthens the extremal-branch/multiplicity route only: it does not prove the complete eigenbasis, global multiplicities, or the matrix condition-number formula (20.19).
- Previous addition: `isSymmetricFiniteMatrix_eigenvectors_sum_mul_eq_zero`, `lsScaledAugmentedMatrix_eigenvectors_sum_mul_eq_zero`, `lsScaledAugmentedMatrix_singularPair_plus_minus_normalized_dot_eq_zero`, `lsScaledAugmentedMatrix_singularPair_plus_leftNull_normalized_dot_eq_zero`, and `lsScaledAugmentedMatrix_singularPair_minus_leftNull_normalized_dot_eq_zero` add the orthogonality dependency for the source-normalized eigenvectors in (20.18).  Distinct eigenvectors of the symmetric scaled augmented matrix are orthogonal, and the printed `lambda_+`, `lambda_-`, and left-nullspace branch vectors are packaged at the source surface.  This advances the spectral multiplicity/decomposition route only: it does not prove the complete eigenbasis, multiplicity count, or the matrix condition-number formula (20.19).
- Previous addition: `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorFormulaRHS_le_etaF_of_formulaRHS_eq_zero`, `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorFormulaRHS_le_etaF_of_isLeastSquaresMinimizer`, and `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorFormulaRHS_le_etaF_of_formulaMatrixRowRank_ne_card` package the zero-branch lower-bound side of (20.21).  Whenever the printed RHS is zero, it is below the nonnegative infimum model `eta_F(y)`; exact minimizers and row-rank-deficient source blocks are exposed as source-facing zero-RHS branches.  This advances the WKS lower-bound handoff for zero branches only: it does not prove the positive-branch Walden--Karlson--Sun spectral lower-bound/attainment theorem, the eigenvalue formula, or the general `theta = infinity` limiting case.
- Previous addition: `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorEtaF_eq_formulaRHS_of_formulaRHS_eq_zero_of_formulaMatrixRowRank_eq_card` and `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorEtaF_eq_formulaRHS_of_formulaRHS_eq_zero_of_left_panel_rowRank_eq_card` package the nondegenerate WKS zero-equality branch.  If the printed RHS in (20.21) is zero and either the source block `[A phi(I-r r^+)]` has full row rank or the original data matrix `A` has full row rank, then `eta_F(y)` equals the printed RHS.  This is still a zero-branch consequence only: it does not prove the positive-branch Walden--Karlson--Sun spectral lower/attainment theorem, the eigenvalue formula, or the general `theta = infinity` limiting case.
- Previous addition: `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorFormulaRHS_eq_zero_iff_etaF_eq_zero_of_theta_pos_of_y_ne_zero_of_left_panel_rowRank_eq_card` and `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorFormulaRHS_pos_iff_etaF_pos_of_theta_pos_of_y_ne_zero_of_left_panel_rowRank_eq_card` package the full-left-panel WKS branch at the source-data RHS surface.  For positive finite `theta`, nonzero `y`, and full row rank of `A`, printed-RHS zero is equivalent to `eta_F(y)=0`, and printed-RHS positivity is equivalent to `eta_F(y)>0`.  This reuses the source-block full-row-rank inheritance theorem and is still a rank-branch result only: it does not prove the full Walden--Karlson--Sun spectral lower/attainment theorem, the eigenvalue formula, or the general `theta = infinity` limiting case.
- Previous addition: `lsRealRectRowRank_leftNull_eq_zero_of_rowRank_eq_card` and `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorFormulaMatrixRowRank_eq_card_of_left_panel_rowRank_eq_card` add the full-left-panel row-rank branch for the WKS source block in (20.21).  A full-row-rank original data matrix `A` has no real left-null vector, and the appended block `[A phi(I-r r^+)]` inherits full row rank from its left `A` panel.  Consequently, the existing full-row-rank nondegenerate branch can be applied whenever `A` itself has full row rank.  This is still a rank-branch closure only: it does not prove the full Walden--Karlson--Sun spectral lower/attainment theorem, the eigenvalue formula, or the general `theta = infinity` limiting case.
- Previous addition: `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorFormulaRHS_eq_zero_iff_etaF_eq_zero_or_formulaMatrixRowRank_ne_card_of_theta_pos_of_y_ne_zero` and `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorFormulaRHS_pos_iff_etaF_pos_and_formulaMatrixRowRank_eq_card_of_theta_pos_of_y_ne_zero` package the finite positive-`theta`, nonzero-`y` WKS branch split directly at the `eta_F`/row-rank surface.  The printed RHS is zero exactly when `eta_F(y)=0` or `[A phi(I-r r^+)]` is row-rank deficient, and it is positive exactly when `eta_F(y)>0` and the same source block has full row rank.  This is still a branch-classification result only: it does not prove the full Walden--Karlson--Sun spectral lower/attainment theorem, the eigenvalue formula, or the general `theta = infinity` limiting case.
- Previous addition: `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorFormulaMatrixSigmaMin_eq_zero_iff_rowRank_ne_card`, `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorFormulaRHS_eq_zero_of_formulaMatrixRowRank_ne_card`, `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorFormulaRHS_eq_zero_iff_formulaMatrixRowRank_ne_card_of_not_isLeastSquaresMinimizer`, `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorFormulaRHS_pos_iff_formulaMatrixRowRank_eq_card_of_not_isLeastSquaresMinimizer`, `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorFormulaRHS_eq_zero_iff_isLeastSquaresMinimizer_or_formulaMatrixRowRank_ne_card_of_theta_pos_of_y_ne_zero`, and `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorFormulaRHS_pos_iff_not_isLeastSquaresMinimizer_and_formulaMatrixRowRank_eq_card_of_theta_pos_of_y_ne_zero` package the row-rank-deficient side of the finite positive-`theta`, nonzero-`y` WKS branch decomposition.  The row-side `sigma_min` branch is zero exactly when `[A phi(I-r r^+)]` is not full row rank; consequently, the printed RHS is zero on the row-rank-deficient branch and is positive exactly on the non-minimizer/full-row-rank branch.  This is still a branch-classification result only: it does not prove the full Walden--Karlson--Sun spectral lower/attainment theorem, the eigenvalue formula, or the general `theta = infinity` limiting case.
- Previous addition: `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorFormulaRHS_eq_zero_iff_etaF_eq_zero_of_theta_pos_of_y_ne_zero_of_formulaMatrixRowRank_eq_card` and `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorFormulaRHS_pos_iff_etaF_pos_of_theta_pos_of_y_ne_zero_of_formulaMatrixRowRank_eq_card` package the finite positive-`theta`, nonzero-`y`, full-row-rank WKS nondegenerate branch.  Under full row rank of the source block `[A phi(I-r r^+)]`, the row-side `sigma_min` branch is positive, so the printed RHS vanishes exactly when `eta_F(y)` vanishes and is positive exactly when `eta_F(y)` is positive.  This is still a source-facing nondegenerate branch bridge only: it avoids the row-rank-deficient branch and does not prove the full Walden--Karlson--Sun spectral lower/attainment theorem, the eigenvalue formula, or the general `theta = infinity` limiting case.
- Previous addition: `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorFormulaRHS_eq_zero_iff_etaF_eq_zero_of_theta_pos_of_y_ne_zero_of_sigmaMin_pos` and `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorFormulaRHS_pos_iff_etaF_pos_of_theta_pos_of_y_ne_zero_of_sigmaMin_pos` package the finite positive-`theta`, nonzero-`y`, positive-row-`sigma_min` WKS nondegenerate branch.  On this branch, the printed RHS vanishes exactly when `eta_F(y)` vanishes, and is positive exactly when `eta_F(y)` is positive.  This is still a branch bridge only: it avoids the degenerate `sigma_min = 0` branch and does not prove the full Walden--Karlson--Sun spectral lower/attainment theorem, the eigenvalue formula, or the general `theta = infinity` limiting case.
- Previous addition: `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorFormulaRHS_eq_zero_iff_isLeastSquaresMinimizer_or_sigmaMin_eq_zero_of_theta_pos_of_y_ne_zero` and `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorFormulaRHS_pos_iff_not_isLeastSquaresMinimizer_and_sigmaMin_pos_of_theta_pos_of_y_ne_zero` package the finite positive-`theta`, nonzero-`y` WKS branch decomposition.  The printed RHS is zero exactly when `y` is an exact least-squares minimizer or the row-side `sigma_min [A phi(I-r r^+)]` branch is degenerate, and it is positive exactly when `y` is not a minimizer and that row-side `sigma_min` is positive.  This records the global branch split at the source-data RHS surface; it does not prove the full Walden--Karlson--Sun spectral lower/attainment theorem, the eigenvalue formula, or the general `theta = infinity` limiting case.
- Previous addition: `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorPhi_pos_of_not_isLeastSquaresMinimizer_of_theta_pos_of_y_ne_zero`, `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorFormulaRHS_eq_zero_iff_sigmaMin_eq_zero_of_not_isLeastSquaresMinimizer`, and `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorFormulaRHS_pos_iff_sigmaMin_pos_of_not_isLeastSquaresMinimizer` classify the remaining finite positive-`theta` WKS zero cases for non-minimizers.  With `y ≠ 0` and `y` not an exact least-squares minimizer, the scalar `phi` branch is positive, so the printed RHS vanishes exactly when the row-side `sigma_min [A phi(I-r r^+)]` vanishes and is positive exactly when that row-side `sigma_min` is positive.  This identifies the degenerate `sigma_min = 0` branch as the only remaining RHS-zero obstruction for non-minimizers; it does not prove the full Walden--Karlson--Sun spectral lower/attainment theorem, the eigenvalue formula, or the general `theta = infinity` limiting case.
- Previous addition: `GQRAQTallAssocCase`, `GQRAQWideAssocCase`, `GQRAQTallAssocCase.mulVec_eq`, `GQRAQWideAssocCase.mulVec_eq`, `GeneralizedQRFactorization.exists_of_tall_qr_assoc_case`, and `GeneralizedQRFactorization.exists_of_wide_qr_assoc_case` add associated-row/associated-column (20.28) shape records and construction wrappers.  They consume supplied `Uᵀ(AQ)=[0;L]` or `Uᵀ(AQ)=[X L]` shape records in the same row/column associations used by (20.27), then reuse the existing supplied-QR packaging theorems to produce `GeneralizedQRFactorization` data.  This is exact supplied-shape algebra only and does not construct `Q`, construct `U`, or prove stability.
- Previous addition: `gqrAQTallBlockAssoc_mulVec` and `gqrAQWideBlockAssoc_mulVec` add vector-action forms for the associated-row/associated-column (20.28) blocks used by the GQR construction wrappers.  They expose how the tall `[0;L]` and wide `[X L]` shapes act on vectors with the same row and column associations as (20.27).  This is exact block algebra only and does not construct `Q`, construct `U`, or prove stability.
- Previous addition: `gqrAQWideL11FromEq20_28`, `gqrAQWideL21FromEq20_28`, `gqrAQWideL22FromEq20_28`, `gqrAQWideL22FromEq20_28_lowerTriangular`, `gqrAQBlock_eq_wideBlockAssoc_of_eq20_28`, and `GeneralizedQRFactorization.exists_of_wide_qr_shapes` add the wide-case reverse packaging for Theorem 20.9.  From a supplied exact `Bᵀ` QR identity and a supplied orthogonal `U` with `Uᵀ(AQ)=[X L]`, lower-triangular `L` is split into the (20.27) `L11`, `L21`, `L22` blocks and packaged as supplied GQR data with `S=Rᵀ`.  This still does not construct the QR factors, construct `U`, or prove computed GQR stability.
- Previous addition: `gqrAQTallL11FromEq20_28`, `gqrAQTallL21FromEq20_28`, `gqrAQTallL22FromEq20_28`, `gqrAQTallL22FromEq20_28_lowerTriangular`, `gqrAQBlock_eq_tallBlockAssoc_of_eq20_28`, and `GeneralizedQRFactorization.exists_of_tall_qr_shapes` add the tall-case reverse packaging for Theorem 20.9.  From a supplied exact `Bᵀ` QR identity and a supplied orthogonal `U` with `Uᵀ(AQ)=[0;L]`, lower-triangular `L` is split into the (20.27) `L11`, `L21`, `L22` blocks and packaged as supplied GQR data with `S=Rᵀ`.  This tall-case bridge still does not construct the QR factors, construct `U`, or prove computed GQR stability.
- Previous addition: `isLowerTriangular_matTranspose_of_isUpperTriangular` and `gqrBQBlock_eq_of_transpose_tall_qr` add the first QR-construction bridge for Theorem 20.9: a supplied exact tall QR factorization `Qᵀ Bᵀ = [R;0]` gives the GQR constraint block identity `BQ = [Rᵀ 0]`, and upper-triangular `R` supplies lower-triangular `S = Rᵀ`.  This is still supplied exact QR algebra; it does not construct the QR/GQR factors or handle the `A Q`/`U` reconstruction.
- Previous addition: `GeneralizedQRFactorization.exists_isLSEMinimizer_of_s_l22_kernel_trivial` states the exact supplied-GQR method consequence at the kernel nonsingularity surface after (20.28).  If the supplied triangular blocks `S` and `L22` have trivial kernels, the triangular equations have exact solution variables `y1`, `y2`, and the recovered vector `Q[y1;y2]` is an LSE minimizer.  This is still supplied-factor algebra; it does not construct the GQR factors or derive the (20.28) reconstruction hypotheses from QR.
- Previous addition: `GeneralizedQRFactorization.conditions20_24_iff_s_l22_kernel_trivial` exposes the Theorem 20.9 proof sentence that the local (20.24) assumptions are equivalent to nonsingularity of `S` and `L22` in trivial-kernel form.  Under supplied GQR data, `LSEFullRowRank B ∧ LSENullIntersectionTrivial A B` is equivalent to both displayed triangular blocks having trivial kernel.  This is still supplied-factor algebra; it does not construct the GQR factors or derive the (20.28) reconstruction hypotheses from QR.
- Previous addition: `GeneralizedQRFactorization.lseFullRowRank_iff_s_kernel_trivial` and `GeneralizedQRFactorization.lseFullRowRank_iff_s_diag_ne_zero` expose the Theorem 20.9 proof sentence that `B` has full rank iff the GQR constraint block `S` is nonsingular.  Under supplied GQR data, the local full-row-rank/surjectivity condition for `B` is equivalent to trivial kernel of `S`, and equivalently to nonzero diagonal entries of the supplied lower-triangular `S`.  This is still supplied-factor algebra; it does not construct the GQR factors or derive the (20.28) reconstruction hypotheses from QR.
- Previous addition: `GeneralizedQRFactorization.nullIntersectionTrivial_iff_l22_kernel_trivial` and `GeneralizedQRFactorization.nullIntersectionTrivial_iff_l22_diag_ne_zero_of_s_diag_ne_zero` expose the conditional Theorem 20.9 proof step after (20.28).  Under supplied GQR data and nonsingular/injective `S`, the local condition `null(A) ∩ null(B) = {0}` is equivalent to trivial kernel of `L22`, and equivalently to nonzero diagonal entries of the supplied lower-triangular `L22`.  This formalizes the source proof sentence `AQ₂ = U₂L22` implies the null-intersection/nonsingular-`L22` equivalence, but still does not construct the GQR factors or derive the (20.28) reconstruction hypotheses from QR.
- Previous addition: `GeneralizedQRFactorization.null_B_iff_exists_Q2_coord` and `GeneralizedQRFactorization.A_Q2_zero_iff_L22_zero` expose two exact block-coordinate proof steps in Theorem 20.9.  Under supplied GQR data, injectivity of `S` identifies `null(B)` with the `Q₂` coordinate range, and on that coordinate range `A x = 0` is equivalent to `L22 y₂ = 0`.  This formalizes the source proof's `null(B)=range(Q₂)` and `AQ₂ = U₂L22` algebra, but still does not construct the GQR factors or derive the (20.28) reconstruction hypotheses from QR.
- Previous addition: `lseWeightedMinimizer_exists_unique_lseMinimizer_tendsto_of_inv_mu_sq` adds an existence-and-uniqueness branch-limit wrapper for the method of weighting in (20.26).  Under the local full-row-rank feasibility and null-intersection uniqueness assumptions from (20.24), if exact weighted minimizers have a convergent branch, all weights are nonzero, and `(mu^2)^{-1} -> 0`, then there is a unique exact LSE minimizer `y` and the branch converges to `y`.  This removes the previously supplied exact LSE minimizer from the theorem surface, but still assumes existence/convergence of the selected exact weighted-minimizer branch and does not prove finite-precision weighted-method stability.
- Previous addition: `lseWeightedMinimizer_tendsto_unique_lseMinimizer_of_inv_mu_sq` adds the unique-solution form of the weighting limit in (20.26).  Under the local null-intersection uniqueness condition from (20.24), if exact weighted minimizers have a convergent branch, an exact LSE minimizer `y` is supplied, all weights are nonzero, and `(mu^2)^{-1} -> 0`, then the branch converges to `y`.  This identifies the limit of an already convergent branch with the unique LSE solution; it does not prove existence/continuity of that branch or finite-precision weighted-method stability.
- Previous addition: `lseWeightedMinimizer_tendsto_isLSEMinimizer_of_inv_mu_sq` combines the weighting residual estimate and the conditional limiting handoff for (20.26).  If exact weighted minimizers have a convergent branch, the constrained problem has a feasible comparator, all weights are nonzero, and `(mu^2)^{-1} -> 0`, then the branch limit is an exact LSE minimizer.  This removes the separate vector residual-convergence hypothesis from the previous handoff, but still assumes existence/convergence of a selected exact weighted-minimizer branch and does not prove finite-precision weighted-method stability.
- Previous addition: `lseWeightedMinimizer_tendsto_isLSEMinimizer_of_constraintResidual_tendsto_zero` adds a conditional limiting handoff for the method of weighting in (20.26).  If exact weighted minimizers have a convergent branch and their equality-constraint residual vectors converge to zero, then the branch limit is an exact LSE minimizer.  This proves the optimization-closure step while still assuming branch existence/convergence and residual convergence; it does not prove existence/continuity of a selected minimizer branch or finite-precision weighted-method stability.
- Previous addition: `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorFormulaRHS_pos_iff` and `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorFormulaRHS_eq_zero_iff` lift the printed (20.21) right-hand side branch anatomy from the supplied-residual value to the source-data convention `r = b - A y`.  They prove that the source-data RHS is positive exactly when both displayed branches are positive, and vanishes exactly when either the `phi` branch or the row-side `sigma_min [A phi(I-r r^+)]` branch vanishes.  This is not the Walden--Karlson--Sun equality proof with `eta_F(y)`; the spectral lower/attainment theorem for non-minimizers, eigenvalue branch formula, and general `theta = infinity` limiting formula remain open.
- Previous addition: `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorFormulaRHS_eq_zero_of_isLeastSquaresMinimizer`, `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorFormulaRHS_eq_zero_of_etaF_eq_zero_of_positive_theta`, and `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorEtaF_eq_formulaRHS_of_etaF_eq_zero_of_positive_theta` package the exact-minimizer WKS zero branch for (20.20)-(20.21).  The first theorem proves that every exact least-squares minimizer makes the printed `min {phi, sigma_min [A phi(I-r r^+)]}` RHS vanish, combining the zero-residual and nonzero-residual left-null branches.  The second theorem proves that, for finite positive `theta`, zero normwise backward error forces that printed RHS to vanish; the third packages the corresponding zero-error equality `eta_F(y) = RHS`.  This still does not prove the converse `RHS = 0 -> eta_F(y) = 0` direction, the general Walden--Karlson--Sun lower/attainment theorem for non-minimizers, the eigenvalue branch formula, or the `theta = infinity` limiting case.

Additional (20.20) increment: `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorEtaF_exists_feasible_cost_lt_add_eps` was added and checked.  Validation: `lake env lean LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/LeastSquares/LSQRSolve.lean`, `lake build LeanFpAnalysis.FP.Algorithms.LeastSquares.LSQRSolve`, the focused `#print axioms` check, the full `LeanFpAnalysis/FP` placeholder scan, `git diff --check`, touched-file whitespace/conflict scans, and full `lake build` passed; the axiom check reports only `propext`, `Classical.choice`, and `Quot.sound`.  This proves epsilon-near feasible costs for the infimum model, not minimum-attainment.

Additional (20.20) increment: `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorEtaF_exists_feasible_deltab_norm_lt_add_eps_div_theta` was added and checked.  Validation: `lake env lean LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/LeastSquares/LSQRSolve.lean`, `lake build LeanFpAnalysis.FP.Algorithms.LeastSquares.LSQRSolve`, the focused `#print axioms` check, the full `LeanFpAnalysis/FP` placeholder scan, `git diff --check`, touched-file whitespace/conflict scans, and full `lake build` passed; the axiom check reports only `propext`, `Classical.choice`, and `Quot.sound`.  This proves a positive finite-`theta` epsilon-near feasible perturbation whose right-hand-side perturbation obeys `||Delta b||_2 < (eta_F(y)+eps)/theta`, not the `theta = infinity` formula or (20.21).

Additional (20.20) increment: `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorCostF_mono_theta_nonneg` and `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorEtaF_mono_theta_nonneg` were added and checked.  Validation: `lake env lean LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/LeastSquares/LSQRSolve.lean`, `lake build LeanFpAnalysis.FP.Algorithms.LeastSquares.LSQRSolve`, the focused `#print axioms` checks, the full `LeanFpAnalysis/FP` placeholder scan, `git diff --check`, touched-file whitespace/conflict scans, and full `lake build` passed; the axiom checks report only `propext`, `Classical.choice`, and `Quot.sound`.  These prove finite nonnegative-`theta` monotonicity of the weighted perturbation cost and the `eta_F` infimum, not the limiting `theta = infinity` formula.

Additional (20.20) increment: `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorCostF_deltaA_le` and `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorEtaF_exists_feasible_deltaA_norm_lt_add_eps` were added and checked.  Validation: `lake env lean LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/LeastSquares/LSQRSolve.lean`, `lake build LeanFpAnalysis.FP.Algorithms.LeastSquares.LSQRSolve`, the focused `#print axioms` checks, the full `LeanFpAnalysis/FP` placeholder scan, `git diff --check`, touched-file whitespace/conflict scans, and full `lake build` passed; the axiom checks report only `propext`, `Classical.choice`, and `Quot.sound`.  These prove finite weighted-cost control of the matrix perturbation and an epsilon-near feasible `DeltaA` norm bound for the infimum model, not minimum-attainment or (20.21).

Additional Theorem 20.4 increment: `LSAsymmetricAugmentedSystem`, `LSAsymmetricAugmentedSystem.symmetric_iff`, `lsQRTallBlock_add`, `LSAsymmetricAugmentedSystem.transformed_qr_solution_of_top_perturbations`, `LSAsymmetricAugmentedSystem.of_transformed_orthogonal`, and `LSAsymmetricAugmentedSystem.exact_qr_solution_of_perturbed_triangular_solves` were added to model the exact asymmetric handoff in Theorem 20.4.  Supplied perturbed triangular equations for the transformed `[R;0]` system now lift through an exact orthogonal factorization to two different perturbations of the two appearances of `A`; later rows supply the concrete RHS-transform/forward-back triangular-solve handoffs, while the matrix QR perturbation remains open.  Validation: `lake env lean LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/LeastSquares/LSQRSolve.lean`, `lake build LeanFpAnalysis.FP.Algorithms.LeastSquares.LSQRSolve`, focused `#print axioms` checks, the full `LeanFpAnalysis/FP` placeholder scan, `git diff --check`, touched-file whitespace/conflict scans, and full `lake build` passed; the axiom checks report only `propext`, `Classical.choice`, and `Quot.sound`, and the full build reported only pre-existing unrelated QR/FastMatMul linter warnings.

Additional Theorem 20.4 increment: `LSAsymmetricAugmentedSystem.exists_exact_qr_solution_of_fl_forwardSub_fl_backSub` was added to instantiate the two triangular solves in the augmented-system QR method with concrete repository algorithms.  It uses `forwardSub_backward_error` for `R^T h=g` and `backSub_backward_error` for `R x=d1+Deltaf1-h`, yielding `gamma fp n` top-block perturbation bounds and the original-coordinate asymmetric augmented system.  This is not the full Theorem 20.4 result: exact QR factors and the transformed-RHS perturbation are still supplied.  Validation: `lake env lean LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/LeastSquares/LSQRSolve.lean`, `lake build LeanFpAnalysis.FP.Algorithms.LeastSquares.LSQRSolve`, the focused `#print axioms` check, the full `LeanFpAnalysis/FP` placeholder scan, `git diff --check`, touched-file whitespace/conflict scans, and full `lake build` passed; the axiom check reports only `propext`, `Classical.choice`, and `Quot.sound`, and the full build reported only pre-existing unrelated QR/FastMatMul linter warnings.

Additional Theorem 20.4 increment: `LSAsymmetricAugmentedSystem.exists_exact_qr_solution_of_fl_forwardSub_fl_backSub_transformed_rhs` was added to construct the original-coordinate RHS perturbation for the triangular-solve handoff.  From exact `Q^T f = [d1; d2]`, it realizes the transformed top perturbation as `Deltaf = Q [Deltaf1; 0]`, then composes with the concrete `fl_forwardSub`/`fl_backSub` theorem.  This still does not prove the rounded Householder QR factorization or the computed `Q^T f` perturbation/bounds.  Validation: `lake env lean LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/LeastSquares/LSQRSolve.lean`, `lake build LeanFpAnalysis.FP.Algorithms.LeastSquares.LSQRSolve`, the focused `#print axioms` check, the full `LeanFpAnalysis/FP` placeholder scan, `git diff --check`, touched-file whitespace/conflict scans, and full `lake build` passed; the axiom check reports only `propext`, `Classical.choice`, and `Quot.sound`, and the full build reported only pre-existing unrelated QR/FastMatMul linter warnings.

Additional Theorem 20.4 increment: `LSAsymmetricAugmentedSystem.exists_exact_qr_solution_of_fl_forwardSub_fl_backSub_of_rhs_explicit_backward_error` and `LSAsymmetricAugmentedSystem.exists_exact_qr_solution_of_fl_forwardSub_fl_backSub_of_fl_householderQRPanel_rhs` were added to connect the QR module's RHS-transform backward-error certificate to the augmented-system solve.  The concrete theorem uses `fl_householderQRPanel_rhs_explicit_backward_error` for the actual transformed RHS recursion, produces an original-coordinate `Deltaf` bounded by `householderQRRhsPanelBackwardBound fp (n+k) n A f`, and composes it with the concrete `fl_forwardSub`/`fl_backSub` handoff.  This still assumes the exact fixed-`Q` relation `A = Q[R;0]`; the matrix QR perturbation and final source theorem remain open.  Validation: `lake env lean LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/LeastSquares/LSQRSolve.lean`, `lake build LeanFpAnalysis.FP.Algorithms.LeastSquares.LSQRSolve`, focused `#print axioms` checks, the full `LeanFpAnalysis/FP` placeholder scan, `git diff --check`, touched-file whitespace/conflict scans, and full `lake build` passed; the axiom checks report only `propext`, `Classical.choice`, and `Quot.sound`, and the full build reported only pre-existing unrelated QR/FastMatMul linter warnings.

Additional Theorem 20.4 increment: `lsQRTallBlock_of_upper_trapezoidal`, `lsQRTallBlock_top_upper_of_upper_trapezoidal`, `LSAsymmetricAugmentedSystem.exists_exact_qr_solution_of_fl_forwardSub_fl_backSub_of_solve_components_fixed_backward_error`, and `LSAsymmetricAugmentedSystem.exists_exact_qr_solution_of_fl_householderQRPanel_solve_components` were added to connect the simultaneous fixed-`Q` QR/RHS panel certificate to the augmented-system solve.  The concrete theorem uses `fl_householderQRPanel_R` and `fl_householderQRPanel_rhs`, extracts the top square `R` block from the upper-trapezoidal tall panel, produces a Frobenius-bounded matrix perturbation `DeltaA`, a bounded original-coordinate `Deltaf`, and the two concrete triangular-solve perturbations.  This Frobenius-bound theorem itself does not prove the printed componentwise `G|A|`, `H_i` bounds or final Theorem 20.4 constants; the later matrix-side Higham wrapper covers the `G|A|` part.  Validation: `lake env lean LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/LeastSquares/LSQRSolve.lean`, `lake build LeanFpAnalysis.FP.Algorithms.LeastSquares.LSQRSolve`, focused `#print axioms` checks, the full `LeanFpAnalysis/FP` placeholder scan, `git diff --check`, touched-file whitespace/conflict scans, and full `lake build` passed; the axiom checks report only `propext`, `Classical.choice`, and `Quot.sound`, and the full build reported only pre-existing unrelated QR/FastMatMul linter warnings.

Additional Theorem 20.4 increment: `LSAsymmetricAugmentedSystem.exists_exact_qr_solution_of_fl_forwardSub_fl_backSub_of_higham_qr_and_rhs_explicit_backward_error` and `LSAsymmetricAugmentedSystem.exists_exact_qr_solution_of_fl_householderQRPanel_higham_matrix_solve_components` were added to upgrade the concrete QR/RHS/triangular handoff with the matrix-side Higham componentwise witness.  The concrete theorem uses `fl_householderQRPanel_R_higham_backward_error_gammaHigham_of_global_gammaValid` and `fl_householderQRPanel_rhs_explicit_backward_error` to return `DeltaA`, a nonnegative `G` with `frobNorm G = 1`, a `|DeltaA| <= ((n+k) * gamma) * G|A|` bound, scalar-bounded `Deltaf`, and the triangular perturbations.  This still does not prove the printed RHS `H_i` bounds or final Theorem 20.4 constants.  Validation: `lake env lean LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/LeastSquares/LSQRSolve.lean`, `lake build LeanFpAnalysis.FP.Algorithms.LeastSquares.LSQRSolve`, focused `#print axioms` checks, the full `LeanFpAnalysis/FP` placeholder scan, `git diff --check`, touched-file whitespace/conflict scans, and full `lake build` passed; the axiom checks report only `propext`, `Classical.choice`, and `Quot.sound`, and the full build reported only pre-existing unrelated QR/FastMatMul linter warnings.

Additional Theorem 20.4 increment: `lsTheorem20_4DeltagMajorant`, `lsTheorem20_4DeltagMajorant_nonneg`, `LSAsymmetricAugmentedSystem.exists_exact_qr_solution_of_fl_forwardSub_fl_backSub_of_higham_qr_and_rhs_explicit_backward_error_with_zero_deltag_bound`, and `LSAsymmetricAugmentedSystem.exists_exact_qr_solution_of_fl_householderQRPanel_higham_matrix_solve_components_with_zero_deltag_bound` were added to expose the printed `Delta g` slot.  The concrete rounded Householder path now returns `Delta g = 0`, a nonnegative Frobenius-unit one-hot `H3`, and the source-shaped bound `|Delta g| <= sqrt(n+k) * n * gamma * |A^T| H3 |rhat|`.  This is not the final Theorem 20.4 RHS result: the printed `Deltaf` bound using `H_1 |f| + H_2 |rhat|` and final theorem package remain open. Additional Theorem 20.4 increment: `lsTheorem20_4DeltafMajorant`, `lsTheorem20_4DeltafMajorant_nonneg`, `LSAsymmetricAugmentedSystem.exists_exact_qr_solution_of_fl_forwardSub_fl_backSub_of_higham_qr_and_rhs_explicit_backward_error_with_source_rhs_bounds`, and `LSAsymmetricAugmentedSystem.exists_exact_qr_solution_of_fl_householderQRPanel_higham_matrix_solve_components_with_source_rhs_bounds` were added to expose the printed `Delta f` source slot conditionally.  The generic and concrete wrappers return `H1`, `H2`, and `H3` with source-shaped `Deltaf` and zero-`Deltag` bounds, while keeping the required domination `hDeltafDom` explicit.  This is not the final Theorem 20.4 RHS proof: the concrete Householder RHS transform still needs a theorem proving `householderQRRhsPanelBackwardBound ... <= sqrt(n+k) * n * gamma * (H1|f| + H2|rhat|)` for suitable nonnegative Frobenius-unit `H1,H2`. This is still not Higham's printed `sqrt(n+k) * n * gamma` coefficient; the remaining work is to sharpen or absorb the closed coefficient under source-visible operation-count/smallness hypotheses.
