Additional (20.21) increment: `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorMu`, `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorPhi`, `lsResidualPseudoinverseProjector`, `lsResidualComplementProjector`, `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorFormulaMatrix`, and the projector action wrappers were added as source-facing foundation for the alternative Walden--Karlson--Sun formula.  They define the source scalars and block matrix, prove `0 <= mu < 1`, `0 <= phi`, model `r r^+` as the nonzero-residual rank-one projector `r r^T / ||r||_2^2`, and prove the block identity `[A phi(I-r r^+)][0;r] = 0`.  This does not prove the `sigma_min` equality, the eigenvalue branch formula, or the `theta = infinity` limiting case.  Validation: `lake env lean LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/LeastSquares/LSQRSolve.lean`, `lake build LeanFpAnalysis.FP.Algorithms.LeastSquares.LSQRSolve`, focused `#print axioms` checks for the new scalar/projector/block theorems, the touched-file placeholder scan, precise conflict-marker and trailing-whitespace scans, `git diff --check`, and full `lake build` passed; the axiom checks report only `propext`, `Classical.choice`, and `Quot.sound`, and the full build reported only pre-existing QR/Givens/FastMatMul linter warnings.

Additional (20.21) increment: `lsVecNorm2Sq_append`, `lsVecNorm2_left_le_of_sum_coords`, `lsVecNorm2_right_le_of_sum_coords`, `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorFormulaMatrix_mulVec`, `lsResidualComplementProjector_rectOpNorm2Le_one`, and `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorFormulaMatrix_rectOpNorm2Le` were added as operator-2 infrastructure for the source block `[A phi(I-r r^+)]`.  They prove finite coordinate-block norm control, the split block action, contraction of `I-r r^+` for nonzero `r`, and the conservative predicate bound `cA + phi` from any rectangular operator-2 bound `cA` for `A`.  This does not prove the `sigma_min` equality, the eigenvalue branch formula, or the `theta = infinity` limiting case.  Validation: `lake env lean LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/LeastSquares/LSQRSolve.lean`, `lake build LeanFpAnalysis.FP.Algorithms.LeastSquares.LSQRSolve`, focused `#print axioms` checks for the new block/operator theorems, the touched-file placeholder scan, precise conflict-marker and trailing-whitespace scans, `git diff --check`, and full `lake build` passed; the axiom checks report only `propext`, `Classical.choice`, and `Quot.sound`, and the full build reported only pre-existing QR/Givens/FastMatMul linter warnings.

Additional (20.21) increment: `lsRealRectRowSingularValue`, `lsRealRectSigmaMinRow`, `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorFormulaMatrixRowSingularValue`, `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorFormulaMatrixSigmaMin`, `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorFormulaMatrixRowSingularValue_le`, and `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorFormulaMatrixSigmaMin_le` were added as the local row-side singular-value API for the source block `[A phi(I-r r^+)]`.  The wrappers use singular values of the complexified transpose for row-side indexing, define nonempty-row `sigma_min` as the last sorted row-side singular value, prove nonnegativity and minimality, and inherit the conservative `cA + phi` bound from the existing block operator certificate.  This does not prove the `eta_F` equality, minimization formula, eigenvalue branch formula, or the `theta = infinity` limiting case.  Validation: `lake env lean LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/LeastSquares/LSQRSolve.lean`, `lake build LeanFpAnalysis.FP.Algorithms.LeastSquares.LSQRSolve`, focused `#print axioms` checks for the new row-side singular-value and `sigma_min` theorems, the touched-file placeholder scan, precise conflict-marker and trailing-whitespace scans, `git diff --check`, and full `lake build` passed; the axiom checks report only `propext`, `Classical.choice`, and `Quot.sound`, and the full build reported only pre-existing QR/Givens/FastMatMul linter warnings.

Additional (20.21) increment: `lsRealRectRowSingularValue_pos_of_sigmaMinRow_pos`, `lsRealRectSigmaMinRow_pos_iff_forall_rowSingularValue_pos`, `lsRealRectSigmaMinRow_eq_zero_iff_exists_rowSingularValue_eq_zero`, `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorFormulaMatrixRowSingularValue_pos_of_sigmaMin_pos`, `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorFormulaMatrixSigmaMin_pos_iff_forall_rowSingularValue_pos`, and `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorFormulaMatrixSigmaMin_eq_zero_iff_exists_rowSingularValue_eq_zero` were added as positivity/zero bridges for the row-side `sigma_min` layer.  They prove that positive row-side `sigma_min` is equivalent to positivity of every row-side singular value, and that zero row-side `sigma_min` is equivalent to the existence of a zero row-side singular value, in both generic real-rectangular and source-block forms.  This does not prove row-rank, the `eta_F` equality, the minimization formula, the eigenvalue branch formula, or the `theta = infinity` limiting case.  Validation: `lake env lean LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/LeastSquares/LSQRSolve.lean`, `lake build LeanFpAnalysis.FP.Algorithms.LeastSquares.LSQRSolve`, focused `#print axioms` checks for the new positivity/zero bridge theorems, the touched-file placeholder scan, precise conflict-marker and trailing-whitespace scans, `git diff --check`, and full `lake build` passed; the axiom checks report only `propext`, `Classical.choice`, and `Quot.sound`, and the full build reported only pre-existing QR/Givens/FastMatMul linter warnings.

Additional (20.21) increment: `lsRealRectRowRank`, `lsRealRectRowRank_eq_card_nonzero_rowSingularValue`, `lsRealRectRowRank_eq_card_of_forall_rowSingularValue_ne_zero`, `lsRealRectRowSingularValue_ne_zero_of_rowRank_eq_card`, `lsRealRectRowRank_eq_card_of_sigmaMinRow_pos`, `lsRealRectSigmaMinRow_pos_of_rowRank_eq_card`, `lsRealRectSigmaMinRow_pos_iff_rowRank_eq_card`, `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorFormulaMatrixRowRank`, `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorFormulaMatrixRowRank_eq_card_nonzero_rowSingularValue`, `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorFormulaMatrixRowRank_eq_card_of_sigmaMin_pos`, `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorFormulaMatrixSigmaMin_pos_of_rowRank_eq_card`, and `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorFormulaMatrixSigmaMin_pos_iff_rowRank_eq_card` were added as the row-rank bridge for the row-side singular-value layer.  They expose the existing complex rank/nonzero-singular-value theorem through the real-rectangular row-side API and prove that positive row-side `sigma_min` is equivalent to full row rank, both generically and for the source block `[A phi(I-r r^+)]`.  This does not prove the `eta_F` equality, the minimization formula, the eigenvalue branch formula, or the `theta = infinity` limiting case. The new definitions record the printed outer minimum `min {phi, sigma_min [A phi(I-r r^+)]}` and the source-data specialization with `r = b - Ay`; the new theorems prove nonnegativity, the two immediate upper bounds by `phi` and `sigma_min`, and the exact `eta_F` equality once the missing spectral lower bound and attaining feasible perturbation are supplied.  This does not prove those lower/attainment obligations, the eigenvalue branch formula, or the `theta = infinity` limiting case. Additional Theorem 20.9 associated-shape action increment: `gqrAQTallBlockAssoc_mulVec` and `gqrAQWideBlockAssoc_mulVec` were added as vector-action forms for the associated (20.28) tall/wide blocks used by the construction wrappers.  The tall theorem proves that the associated `[0;L]` block sends `y` to the associated vector `[0; Ly]`; the wide theorem proves that the associated `[X L]` block sends `[y0;y1;y2]` to `X y0 + L [y1;y2]`.  These are exact supplied-shape algebra only: they do not construct `Q`, construct the orthogonal `U`, prove the shape hypotheses from actual factors, or prove computed GQR stability.  Validation: `lake env lean LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/LeastSquares/LSE.lean`, `lake build LeanFpAnalysis.FP.Algorithms.LeastSquares.LSE`, focused `#print axioms` for the two new public associated-shape action theorems, the touched-Lean-file placeholder scan, precise conflict-marker and trailing-whitespace scans, `git diff --check`, and full `lake build` passed; the axiom checks report only `propext`, `Classical.choice`, and `Quot.sound`, and the full build reported only pre-existing GivensSpec/FastMatMul linter warnings.

Additional Theorem 20.9 wide construction increment: `gqrAQWideL11FromEq20_28`, `gqrAQWideL21FromEq20_28`, `gqrAQWideL22FromEq20_28`, `gqrAQWideL22FromEq20_28_lowerTriangular`, `gqrAQBlock_eq_wideBlockAssoc_of_eq20_28`, and `GeneralizedQRFactorization.exists_of_wide_qr_shapes` were added as the wide-case reverse packaging bridge.  From supplied exact `B^T` QR data and a supplied exact wide shape `U^T(AQ)=[X L]`, the bridge extracts the (20.27) blocks from the leading block `X` and lower-triangular `L`, proves the extracted `L22` is lower triangular, uses lower-triangularity to justify the top-right zero block, and packages supplied wide-case GQR data with `S=R^T`.  This does not construct `Q`, construct the orthogonal `U`, or prove computed GQR stability.  Validation: `lake env lean LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/LeastSquares/LSE.lean`, `lake build LeanFpAnalysis.FP.Algorithms.LeastSquares.LSE`, focused `#print axioms` for the three new public wide packaging theorems, the touched-Lean-file placeholder scan, precise conflict-marker and trailing-whitespace scans, `git diff --check`, and full `lake build` passed; the axiom checks report only `propext`, `Classical.choice`, and `Quot.sound`, and the full build reported only pre-existing GivensSpec/FastMatMul linter warnings.

From supplied exact `B^T` QR data and a supplied exact tall shape `U^T(AQ)=[0;L]`, the bridge extracts the (20.27) blocks from lower-triangular `L`, proves the extracted `L22` is lower triangular, uses lower-triangularity to justify the top-right zero block, and packages supplied tall-case GQR data with `S=R^T`.  This does not construct `Q`, construct the orthogonal `U`, or prove computed GQR stability. Additional Theorem 20.9 construction increment: `isLowerTriangular_matTranspose_of_isUpperTriangular` and `gqrBQBlock_eq_of_transpose_tall_qr` were added as the first QR-to-GQR construction bridge.  From a supplied exact transformed tall QR identity `Q^T B^T = [R;0]`, the bridge proves `B Q = [R^T 0]`, so the GQR constraint block has `S = R^T`; the triangularity theorem turns upper-triangular `R` into lower-triangular `S`.  This does not construct the QR factors, prove full GQR existence, or handle the transformed-`A` block reconstruction.  Validation: `lake env lean LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/LeastSquares/LSE.lean`, `lake build LeanFpAnalysis.FP.Algorithms.LeastSquares.LSE`, focused `#print axioms` for the two new QR-to-GQR bridge theorems, the touched-Lean-file placeholder scan, precise conflict-marker and trailing-whitespace scans, `git diff --check`, and full `lake build` passed; the axiom checks report only `propext`, `Classical.choice`, and `Quot.sound`, and the full build reported only pre-existing GivensSpec/FastMatMul linter warnings.

Additional (20.27) increment: `GeneralizedQRFactorization.lseFullRowRank_iff_s_kernel_trivial` and `GeneralizedQRFactorization.lseFullRowRank_iff_s_diag_ne_zero` were added as supplied-GQR wrappers for the Theorem 20.9 proof sentence that `B` has full rank iff `S` is nonsingular.  They prove the local full-row-rank/surjectivity condition for `B` is equivalent to trivial kernel of `S`, and equivalently to nonzero diagonal entries of lower-triangular `S`, under supplied GQR data.  This does not construct the GQR factors or prove the (20.28) reconstruction hypotheses from QR. Additional (20.27) increment: `GeneralizedQRFactorization.conditions20_24_iff_s_l22_kernel_trivial` was added as the kernel-form supplied-GQR wrapper for the Theorem 20.9 proof sentence that (20.24) is equivalent to nonsingularity of `S` and `L22`.  It combines the `B`/`S` full-rank kernel bridge with the conditional `L22` kernel bridge under supplied GQR data.  This does not construct the GQR factors or prove the (20.28) reconstruction hypotheses from QR. Additional (20.27) increment: `GeneralizedQRFactorization.exists_isLSEMinimizer_of_s_l22_kernel_trivial` was added as the kernel-surface supplied-GQR method wrapper for Theorem 20.9.  It uses the kernel-form local-(20.24) equivalence to derive exact triangular solve variables `y1`, `y2` and an LSE minimizer from trivial kernels of the supplied `S` and `L22` blocks.  This does not construct the GQR factors, prove the (20.28) reconstruction hypotheses from QR, or prove computed GQR stability. Additional (20.27) increment: `GeneralizedQRFactorization.nullIntersectionTrivial_iff_l22_kernel_trivial` and `GeneralizedQRFactorization.nullIntersectionTrivial_iff_l22_diag_ne_zero_of_s_diag_ne_zero` were added as conditional supplied-GQR proof-step wrappers for Theorem 20.9.  They prove that, under supplied GQR data and nonsingular/injective `S`, the local condition `null(A) ∩ null(B) = {0}` is equivalent to the trivial kernel of `L22`, and equivalently to nonzero diagonal entries of the lower-triangular `L22` block.  This makes the source proof sentence following `null(B)=range(Q₂)` and `AQ₂=U₂L22` explicit, but still assumes supplied GQR data and does not construct the orthogonal factors or prove the (20.28) top-zero/lower-triangular reconstruction hypotheses from the QR construction.  Validation: `lake env lean LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/LeastSquares/LSE.lean`, `lake build LeanFpAnalysis.FP.Algorithms.LeastSquares.LSE`, focused `#print axioms` checks for the two new conditional supplied-GQR proof-step theorems, the touched-Lean-file placeholder scan, precise conflict-marker and trailing-whitespace scans, `git diff --check`, and full `lake build` passed; the axiom checks report only `propext`, `Classical.choice`, and `Quot.sound`, and the full build reported only pre-existing QR/Givens/FastMatMul linter warnings.

Additional (20.27) increment: `GeneralizedQRFactorization.null_B_iff_exists_Q2_coord` and `GeneralizedQRFactorization.A_Q2_zero_iff_L22_zero` were added as named supplied-GQR proof steps for Theorem 20.9.  The first theorem proves the source claim `null(B)=range(Q₂)` from the block identity `BQ=[S 0]` and injectivity of `S`; the second proves that on this `Q₂` coordinate range, `A x = 0` is equivalent to the triangular condition `L22 y₂ = 0`.  This makes the existing (20.24)-to-`S`/`L22` equivalence more source-transparent, but still assumes supplied GQR data and does not construct the orthogonal factors or prove the (20.28) top-zero/lower-triangular reconstruction hypotheses from the QR construction.  Validation: `lake env lean LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/LeastSquares/LSE.lean`, `lake build LeanFpAnalysis.FP.Algorithms.LeastSquares.LSE`, focused `#print axioms` checks for the two new supplied-GQR proof-step theorems, the touched-Lean-file placeholder scan, precise conflict-marker and trailing-whitespace scans, `git diff --check`, and full `lake build` passed; the axiom checks report only `propext`, `Classical.choice`, and `Quot.sound`, and the full build reported only pre-existing QR/Givens/FastMatMul linter warnings.

Additional (20.26) increment: `lseWeightedMinimizer_exists_unique_lseMinimizer_tendsto_of_inv_mu_sq` was added as the (20.24)-based existence-and-uniqueness branch-limit wrapper for the method of weighting.  It proves that local full-row-rank feasibility, null-intersection uniqueness, a supplied convergent exact weighted-minimizer branch, nonzero weights, and `(mu^2)^{-1} -> 0` give a unique exact LSE minimizer and convergence of that branch to it.  This removes the supplied exact constrained minimizer from the theorem surface; it still does not prove existence or continuity of a selected weighted minimizer branch, full convergence without a supplied branch, or finite-precision weighted-method stability.  Validation: `lake env lean LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/LeastSquares/LSE.lean`, `lake build LeanFpAnalysis.FP.Algorithms.LeastSquares.LSE`, focused `#print axioms` for the new existence-and-uniqueness wrapper, the touched-Lean-file placeholder scan, precise conflict-marker and trailing-whitespace scans, `git diff --check`, and full `lake build` passed; the axiom check reports only `propext`, `Classical.choice`, and `Quot.sound`, and the full build reported only pre-existing QR/Givens/FastMatMul linter warnings.

Additional (20.26) increment: `lseWeightedMinimizer_tendsto_unique_lseMinimizer_of_inv_mu_sq` was added as the unique-solution identification wrapper for the method of weighting.  It proves that, under the local null-intersection uniqueness condition from (20.24), any supplied convergent exact weighted-minimizer branch with nonzero weights and `(mu^2)^{-1} -> 0` converges to a supplied exact LSE minimizer `y`.  This composes the inverse-square-weight branch-limit theorem with `IsLSEMinimizer.eq_of_nullIntersectionTrivial`; it still does not prove existence or continuity of a selected weighted minimizer branch, full convergence without a supplied branch, or finite-precision weighted-method stability.  Validation: `lake env lean LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/LeastSquares/LSE.lean`, `lake build LeanFpAnalysis.FP.Algorithms.LeastSquares.LSE`, focused `#print axioms` for the new unique-solution limit wrapper, the touched-Lean-file placeholder scan, precise conflict-marker and trailing-whitespace scans, `git diff --check`, and full `lake build` passed; the axiom check reports only `propext`, `Classical.choice`, and `Quot.sound`, and the full build reported only pre-existing QR/Givens/FastMatMul linter warnings.

Additional (20.26) increment: `lseWeightedMinimizer_tendsto_isLSEMinimizer_of_inv_mu_sq` was added as the combined inverse-square-weight branch-limit theorem for the method of weighting.  It proves that, if exact minimizers of the weighted unconstrained problems have a convergent branch, a feasible constrained comparator exists, all weights are nonzero, and `(mu^2)^{-1} -> 0`, then the branch limit is an exact LSE minimizer.  This removes the previous separate vector residual-convergence assumption by using the proved norm-residual convergence plus finite-dimensional coordinate convergence; it does not prove existence or continuity of a selected weighted minimizer branch, full convergence without a supplied branch, or finite-precision weighted-method stability.  Validation: `lake env lean LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/LeastSquares/LSE.lean`, `lake build LeanFpAnalysis.FP.Algorithms.LeastSquares.LSE`, focused `#print axioms` for the new branch-limit theorem, the touched-Lean-file placeholder scan, precise conflict-marker and trailing-whitespace scans, `git diff --check`, and full `lake build` passed; the axiom check reports only `propext`, `Classical.choice`, and `Quot.sound`, and the full build reported only pre-existing QR/Givens/FastMatMul linter warnings.

Additional (20.26) increment: `lseWeightedMinimizer_tendsto_isLSEMinimizer_of_constraintResidual_tendsto_zero` was added as a conditional optimization-closure theorem for the method of weighting.  It proves that if exact minimizers of the weighted unconstrained problems have a convergent branch and the equality-constraint residual vectors tend to zero, then the branch limit is an exact LSE minimizer.  This does not prove existence or continuity of a selected weighted minimizer branch, automatic vector residual convergence from the norm-residual theorem, full convergence to the unique LSE solution, or finite-precision weighted-method stability.  Validation: `lake env lean LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/LeastSquares/LSE.lean`, `lake build LeanFpAnalysis.FP.Algorithms.LeastSquares.LSE`, focused `#print axioms` for the new conditional limiting theorem, the touched-Lean-file placeholder scan, precise conflict-marker and trailing-whitespace scans, `git diff --check`, and full `lake build` passed; the axiom check reports only `propext`, `Classical.choice`, and `Quot.sound`, and the full build reported only pre-existing QR/Givens/FastMatMul linter warnings.

Additional (20.26) increment: `lseWeightedMinimizer_objective_le_feasibleObjective` and `lseWeightedMinimizer_objective_le_lseMinimizer` were added as finite-weight objective-domination support for the method of weighting.  They prove that any exact minimizer of the weighted unconstrained problem has original least-squares objective no larger than any feasible constrained comparator, and specialize this to an LSE minimizer comparator.  This does not prove convergence as `mu -> infinity`, existence/continuity of a selected minimizer branch, or any finite-precision weighted-method stability theorem.  Validation: `lake env lean LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/LeastSquares/LSE.lean`, `lake build LeanFpAnalysis.FP.Algorithms.LeastSquares.LSE`, focused `#print axioms` checks for the two new finite-weight objective theorems, the touched-Lean-file placeholder scan, precise conflict-marker and trailing-whitespace scans, `git diff --check`, and full `lake build` passed; the axiom checks report only `propext`, `Classical.choice`, and `Quot.sound`, and the full build reported only pre-existing QR/Givens/FastMatMul linter warnings.

Additional (20.26) increment: `lseWeightedConstraintPenalty_le_objective`, `lseConstraintResidual_normSq_le_of_weightedObjective_le`, `lseConstraintResidual_norm_le_sqrt_div_of_weightedObjective_le`, and `lseWeightedMinimizer_constraintResidual_normSq_le_feasibleObjective_div_mu_sq` were added as finite-weight coercivity support for the method of weighting.  They prove that the weighted objective dominates `mu^2 * ||Bx-d||_2^2`, that any candidate with weighted objective at most `R` has constraint residual bounded by `R / mu^2` in squared norm, and that an exact minimizer of the weighted problem inherits such a bound from any feasible constrained comparator.  This does not prove convergence as `mu -> infinity`, existence/continuity of a selected minimizer branch, or any finite-precision weighted-method stability theorem.  Validation: `lake env lean LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/LeastSquares/LSE.lean`, `lake build LeanFpAnalysis.FP.Algorithms.LeastSquares.LSE`, focused `#print axioms` checks for the four new finite-weight coercivity theorems, the touched-Lean-file placeholder scan, precise conflict-marker scan, `git diff --check`, and full `lake build` passed; the axiom checks report only `propext`, `Classical.choice`, and `Quot.sound`, and the full build reported only pre-existing QR/Givens/FastMatMul linter warnings.

Additional (20.26) increment: `lseWeightedMinimizer_constraintResidual_normSq_tendsto_zero_of_inv_mu_sq` and `lseWeightedMinimizer_constraintResidual_norm_tendsto_zero_of_inv_mu_sq` were added as the first limiting ingredient for the method of weighting.  They use the finite-weight minimizer bound plus a real squeeze argument to prove that if exact weighted minimizers are available for nonzero weights and `(mu^2)^{-1}` tends to zero, then the equality-constraint residual tends to zero in squared norm and norm.  This does not prove convergence of the weighted minimizers to the LSE solution, existence/continuity of a selected minimizer branch, or any finite-precision weighted-method stability theorem.  Validation: `lake env lean LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/LeastSquares/LSE.lean`, `lake build LeanFpAnalysis.FP.Algorithms.LeastSquares.LSE`, focused `#print axioms` checks for the two new sequence-level feasibility-convergence theorems, the touched-Lean-file placeholder scan, precise conflict-marker scan, `git diff --check`, and full `lake build` passed; the axiom checks report only `propext`, `Classical.choice`, and `Quot.sound`, and the full build reported only pre-existing QR/Givens/FastMatMul linter warnings.

Additional (20.17)-(20.19) increment: `lsScaledAugmentedMatrix_zero_quadraticForm_eq_reindexed_rectSelfAdjointDilation`, `lsScaledAugmentedMatrix_zero_finiteLoewnerLe_scalar_id_of_rectOpNorm2Le`, and `lsScaledAugmentedMatrix_zero_neg_finiteLoewnerLe_scalar_id_of_rectOpNorm2Le` were added as source-indexed quadratic-form and Loewner-control bridges for the zero-scaled augmented matrix.  The arbitrary-vector Rayleigh theorem reindexes any `Fin (m+n)` source vector into the repository self-adjoint-dilation vector and proves equality of the quadratic forms; the two Loewner corollaries transfer existing rectangular operator-2 control of `A` to the source inequalities `C(0) <= L I` and `-C(0) <= L I`.  This gives the spectral route a two-sided source-surface bound, but it does not prove the sharp spectral norm, the global spectral multiplicities in (20.18), a spectral decomposition, the actual matrix condition-number bridge for `C(alpha)`, or a full `min_alpha`/`max_alpha` theorem.  Validation: `lake env lean LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/LeastSquares/LSQRSolve.lean`, `lake build LeanFpAnalysis.FP.Algorithms.LeastSquares.LSQRSolve`, focused `#print axioms` checks for the three new public theorems, the touched-Lean-file placeholder scan, precise conflict-marker scan, `git diff --check`, and full `lake build` passed; the axiom checks report only `propext`, `Classical.choice`, and `Quot.sound`, and the full build reported only pre-existing QR/Givens/FastMatMul warnings.

Additional (20.17)-(20.19) increment: `rectSelfAdjointDilation_finiteOpNorm2Le_of_lsScaledAugmentedMatrix_zero`, `lsScaledAugmentedMatrix_zero_finiteOpNorm2Le_iff_rectSelfAdjointDilation`, and `lsScaledAugmentedMatrix_zero_finiteOpNorm2Le_of_frobNormRect_le` were added as the bidirectional finite-operator-bound bridge for the zero-scaled augmented matrix.  The new converse transfers any source-indexed `C(0)` finite operator-2 bound back to the repository self-adjoint dilation; the iff wrapper records equivalence of the two bound predicates under the existing reindexing; and the Frobenius corollary gives a conservative `sqrt 2 * L` source-surface bound from `frobNormRect A <= L`.  This does not prove the sharp spectral norm, the global spectral multiplicities in (20.18), a spectral decomposition, the actual matrix condition-number bridge for `C(alpha)`, or a full `min_alpha`/`max_alpha` theorem. Additional (20.17)-(20.19) increment: `lsScaledAugmentedMatrix_zero_finiteOpNorm2Le_of_rectSelfAdjointDilation` was added as the zero-scaling operator-bound bridge for the scaled augmented matrix.  It proves that any finite operator-2 upper bound for the repository's `rectSelfAdjointDilation A` transfers to the source-indexed `C(0)` matrix over `Fin (m+n)`, giving the later spectral and condition-number route a direct source-surface operator-bound handoff.  This does not prove the sharp operator norm, the global spectral multiplicities in (20.18), a spectral decomposition, the actual matrix condition-number bridge for `C(alpha)`, or a full `min_alpha`/`max_alpha` theorem.  Validation: `lake env lean LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/LeastSquares/LSQRSolve.lean`, `lake build LeanFpAnalysis.FP.Algorithms.LeastSquares.LSQRSolve`, focused `#print axioms` for the new theorem, the touched-Lean-file placeholder scan, precise conflict-marker scan, `git diff --check`, and full `lake build` passed; the axiom check reports only `propext`, `Classical.choice`, and `Quot.sound`, and the full build reported only pre-existing QR/Givens/FastMatMul warnings.

Additional (20.17)-(20.19) increment: `lsScaledAugmentedMatrix_zero_quadraticForm_append_eq` was added as the zero-scaling Rayleigh-form bridge for the scaled augmented matrix.  It proves that `([r;x])^T C(0) [r;x] = 2 r^T A x` from (20.17), indexed by `Fin (m+n)`, giving the later spectral and operator-norm route a direct source-indexed quadratic-form connection to the repository's self-adjoint-dilation infrastructure.  This does not prove the global spectral multiplicities in (20.18), a spectral decomposition, an operator-norm equality, the actual matrix condition-number bridge for `C(alpha)`, or a full `min_alpha`/`max_alpha` theorem. Additional (20.17)-(20.19) increment: `lsScaledAugmentedMatrix_zero_mulVec_eq_reindexed_rectSelfAdjointDilation_sumBothVec` was added as the zero-scaling vector-action bridge for the scaled augmented matrix.  It proves that `C(0) * [r;x]` from (20.17), indexed by `Fin (m+n)`, is exactly the `finSumFinEquiv` reindexing of the repository's `finiteMatVec (rectSelfAdjointDilation A) (sumBothVec r x)`, indexed by `Fin m ⊕ Fin n`.  This gives the later spectral and operator-norm route a direct action-level connection to existing self-adjoint-dilation infrastructure; it does not prove the global spectral multiplicities in (20.18), a spectral decomposition, an operator-norm equality, the actual matrix condition-number bridge for `C(alpha)`, or a full `min_alpha`/`max_alpha` theorem. Additional (20.17)-(20.19) increment: `lsScaledAugmentedMatrix_zero_eq_reindexed_rectSelfAdjointDilation` was added as the zero-scaling bridge for the scaled augmented matrix.  It proves that `C(0) = [[0, A], [A^T, 0]]` from (20.17), indexed by `Fin (m+n)`, is exactly the `finSumFinEquiv` reindexing of the repository's `rectSelfAdjointDilation A`, indexed by `Fin m ⊕ Fin n`.  This gives the later spectral route a direct connection to existing self-adjoint-dilation infrastructure; it does not prove the global spectral multiplicities in (20.18), a spectral decomposition, an operator-norm equality, the actual matrix condition-number bridge for `C(alpha)`, or a full `min_alpha`/`max_alpha` theorem.  Validation: `lake env lean LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/LeastSquares/LSQRSolve.lean`, `lake build LeanFpAnalysis.FP.Algorithms.LeastSquares.LSQRSolve`, focused `#print axioms` for the new theorem, the touched-Lean-file placeholder scan, precise conflict-marker scan, `git diff --check`, and full `lake build` passed; the axiom check reports only `propext`, `Classical.choice`, and `Quot.sound`, and the full build reported only pre-existing QR/Givens/FastMatMul linter warnings.

Additional (20.17)-(20.19) increment: `lsScaledAugmentedMatrix_symmetric` was added as the matrix-side symmetry bridge for the scaled augmented matrix `C(alpha) = [[alpha I, A], [A^T, 0]]`.  It proves the repository-native `IsSymmetricFiniteMatrix` predicate for the exact block matrix from (20.17), giving a required prerequisite for the later spectral/eigenvalue and condition-number bridge.  This does not prove the global spectral multiplicities in (20.18), a spectral decomposition, the actual matrix condition-number bridge for `C(alpha)`, or a full `min_alpha`/`max_alpha` theorem.  Validation: `lake env lean LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/LeastSquares/LSQRSolve.lean` passed before and after the change; full milestone validation is recorded for this commit.

Additional (20.19) increment: `lsScaledAugmentedBranchRatio_gt_sigma_ratio_sq_of_alpha_eq_sigmaMax` was added as a scalar certificate for the "worse than `kappa_2(A)^2`" side of the scaled augmented matrix discussion.  It proves that when `0 < sigmaMin <= sigmaMax` and `alpha = sigmaMax`, the extremal branch ratio `lambda_+(sigmaMax) / |lambda_-(sigmaMin)|` is strictly larger than `(sigmaMax / sigmaMin)^2`.  This does not prove the global spectral multiplicities in (20.18), the actual matrix condition-number bridge for `C(alpha)`, or a full `max_alpha` theorem.  Validation: `lake env lean LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/LeastSquares/LSQRSolve.lean`, `lake build LeanFpAnalysis.FP.Algorithms.LeastSquares.LSQRSolve`, focused `#print axioms` check for `lsScaledAugmentedBranchRatio_gt_sigma_ratio_sq_of_alpha_eq_sigmaMax`, the touched-Lean-file placeholder scan, precise conflict-marker scan, `git diff --check`, and full `lake build` passed; the axiom check reports only `propext`, `Classical.choice`, and `Quot.sound`, and the full build reported only pre-existing QR/Givens/FastMatMul linter warnings.

Additional (20.20)-(20.21) increment: `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorEtaF_eq_zero_iff_isLeastSquaresMinimizer_of_positive_theta`, `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorEtaF_pos_iff_not_isLeastSquaresMinimizer_of_positive_theta`, `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorFormulaValue_pos_iff`, and `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorFormulaValue_eq_zero_iff` were added as zero/positivity bridges.  For positive finite `theta`, the first two prove that the normwise backward-error infimum `eta_F(y)` vanishes exactly when `y` is already an exact least-squares minimizer, and is positive exactly when it is not.  The latter two prove that the printed (20.21) right-hand side `min {phi, sigma_min [A phi(I-r r^+)]}` is positive exactly when both displayed branches are positive, and vanishes exactly when one branch vanishes.  This does not prove the Walden--Karlson--Sun spectral lower bound, construct an attaining feasible perturbation, prove equality with `eta_F`, derive the eigenvalue branch formula, or handle the `theta = infinity` limiting case.  Validation: `lake env lean LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/LeastSquares/LSQRSolve.lean`, `lake build LeanFpAnalysis.FP.Algorithms.LeastSquares.LSQRSolve`, focused `#print axioms` checks for the four new zero/positivity bridge theorems, the touched-Lean-file placeholder scan, precise conflict-marker scan, `git diff --check`, and full `lake build` passed; the axiom checks report only `propext`, `Classical.choice`, and `Quot.sound`, and the full build reported only pre-existing QR/Givens/FastMatMul linter warnings.

Additional (20.20)-(20.21) increment: `IsLeastSquaresMinimizer.of_lsResidualHigham_eq_zero`, `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorPhi_eq_zero_of_residual_eq_zero`, `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorFormulaRHS_eq_zero_of_lsResidualHigham_eq_zero`, and `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorEtaF_eq_formulaRHS_of_lsResidualHigham_eq_zero` were added as the exact zero-residual branch of the normwise backward-error formula.  They prove that if the source residual `b - A y` is zero, then `y` is an exact least-squares minimizer, the scalar `phi` branch and the full printed (20.21) RHS vanish, and `eta_F(y)` equals the printed RHS without using the missing general WKS spectral certificate.  This does not prove the nonzero-residual lower bound, construct an attaining feasible perturbation, derive the eigenvalue branch formula, or handle the `theta = infinity` limiting case.  Validation: `lake env lean LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/LeastSquares/LSQRSolve.lean`, `lake build LeanFpAnalysis.FP.Algorithms.LeastSquares.LSQRSolve`, focused `#print axioms` checks for the four new exact-zero-residual branch theorems, the touched-Lean-file placeholder scan, precise conflict-marker scan, `git diff --check`, and full `lake build` passed; the axiom checks report only `propext`, `Classical.choice`, and `Quot.sound`, and the full build reported only pre-existing QR/Givens/FastMatMul linter warnings.

Additional (20.21) increment: `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorFormulaMatrix_left_residual_col_sum_eq_zero`, `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorFormulaMatrix_right_residual_col_sum_eq_zero`, `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorFormulaMatrix_residual_leftNull_of_column_orthogonal`, and `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorFormulaMatrix_residual_leftNull_of_isLeastSquaresMinimizer` were added as the nonzero-residual left-null foundation for the WKS source block.  They prove that if a supplied residual is orthogonal to the data columns and nonzero, then it annihilates `[A phi(I-r r^+)]` from the left; in particular, an exact LS minimizer with nonzero Higham residual satisfies `r^T [A phi(I-r r^+)] = 0`.  This was a left-null-only foundation; the following increment adds row-rank deficiency and zero `sigma_min` for exact-minimizer branches, while the general lower-bound/attainment proof, eigenvalue formula, and `theta = infinity` remain open.  Validation: `lake env lean LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/LeastSquares/LSQRSolve.lean`, `lake build LeanFpAnalysis.FP.Algorithms.LeastSquares.LSQRSolve`, focused `#print axioms` checks for the four new left-null theorems, the touched-Lean-file placeholder scan, precise conflict-marker scan, `git diff --check`, and full `lake build` passed; the axiom checks report only `propext`, `Classical.choice`, and `Quot.sound`, and the full build reported only pre-existing QR/Givens/FastMatMul linter warnings.

Additional (20.21) increment: `lsRealRectRowRank_ne_card_of_leftNull`, `lsRealRectSigmaMinRow_eq_zero_of_leftNull`, `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorFormulaMatrixRowRank_ne_card_of_leftNull`, `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorFormulaMatrixSigmaMin_eq_zero_of_leftNull`, `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorFormulaMatrixSigmaMin_eq_zero_of_isLeastSquaresMinimizer`, `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorFormulaRHS_eq_zero_of_isLeastSquaresMinimizer_nonzero_residual`, and `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorEtaF_eq_formulaRHS_of_isLeastSquaresMinimizer` were added as the left-null-to-row-rank and exact-minimizer residual-branch closure for the WKS source block.  A nonzero vector annihilating every source-block column rules out full row rank and forces the row-side `sigma_min` branch to vanish; for an exact LS minimizer with nonzero residual this makes the printed RHS zero, and together with the existing zero-residual branch proves `eta_F(y)` equals the printed RHS for every exact LS minimizer.  This does not prove the general lower-bound/attainment theorem for non-minimizers, the eigenvalue branch formula, or `theta = infinity`.  Validation: `lake env lean LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/LeastSquares/LSQRSolve.lean`, `lake build LeanFpAnalysis.FP.Algorithms.LeastSquares.LSQRSolve`, focused `#print axioms` checks for the seven new public theorems, the touched-Lean-file placeholder scan, precise conflict-marker scan, `git diff --check`, and full `lake build` passed; the axiom checks report only `propext`, `Classical.choice`, and `Quot.sound`, and the full build reported only pre-existing QR/Givens/FastMatMul linter warnings.

Additional (20.20)-(20.21) increment: `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorFormulaRHS_eq_zero_of_isLeastSquaresMinimizer` and `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorFormulaRHS_eq_zero_of_etaF_eq_zero_of_positive_theta` were added as source-facing WKS zero-branch wrappers.  The first theorem packages both exact-minimizer residual branches into the printed right-hand side itself: if `y` is an exact least-squares minimizer, then `min {phi, sigma_min [A phi(I-r r^+)]} = 0`.  The second theorem combines the finite positive-`theta` zero-error characterization with that wrapper, proving that `eta_F(y) = 0` forces the printed RHS to vanish.  This does not prove a converse for all RHS-zero cases, the general Walden--Karlson--Sun lower-bound/attainment theorem for non-minimizers, the eigenvalue branch formula, or the `theta = infinity` limiting case.  Validation: `lake env lean LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/LeastSquares/LSQRSolve.lean`, `lake build LeanFpAnalysis.FP.Algorithms.LeastSquares.LSQRSolve`, focused `#print axioms` checks for the two new public theorems, the touched-Lean-file placeholder scan, precise conflict-marker scan, `git diff --check`, and full `lake build` passed; the axiom checks report only `propext`, `Classical.choice`, and `Quot.sound`, and the full build reported only pre-existing QR/Givens/FastMatMul warnings.

Additional (20.18) increment: `lsScaledAugmentedMatrix_singularPair_plus_minus_normalized_coefficients_eq_zero` and `lsScaledAugmentedMatrix_singularPair_plus_minus_leftNull_normalized_coefficients_eq_zero` were added as source-normalized coefficient-independence certificates for the printed scaled-augmented eigenvector shapes.  The two theorems transfer the existing unnormalized independence results for `[lambda_\pm u; sigma v]` to `[u; (sigma/lambda_\pm)v]`, and separate those printed singular-pair branches from a nonzero left-null branch `[w;0]`.  This is local multiplicity infrastructure only: it does not construct a complete singular-vector/nullspace basis, prove the global spectral decomposition, count source multiplicities, or connect the branches to the matrix condition number in (20.19).  Validation: `lake env lean LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/LeastSquares/LSQRSolve.lean`, `lake build LeanFpAnalysis.FP.Algorithms.LeastSquares.LSQRSolve`, focused `#print axioms` checks for the two new public theorems, the touched-Lean-file placeholder scan, precise conflict-marker scan, `git diff --check`, and full `lake build` passed; the axiom checks report only `propext`, `Classical.choice`, and `Quot.sound`, and the full build reported only pre-existing QR/Givens/FastMatMul warnings.

Additional (20.18) increment: `lsScaledAugmentedMatrix_singularPair_plus_normalized_eigenpair` and `lsScaledAugmentedMatrix_singularPair_minus_normalized_eigenpair` were added as packaged source-normalized nonzero eigenpair certificates for the scaled augmented matrix.  The shared generic theorem `lsScaledAugmentedMatrix_singularPair_normalized_eigenvector_of_quadratic` proves the printed vector shape `[u; (sigma/lambda) v]` whenever `lambda != 0` and satisfies the displayed quadratic; the plus and minus wrappers instantiate it with `lambda_+` and `lambda_-` under `alpha >= 0`, `sigma != 0`, and `v != 0`.  This gives the later spectral multiplicity and condition-number bridge the source-normalized eigenvector surface, but it does not prove global multiplicities, a spectral decomposition, or the matrix condition-number formula.  Validation: `lake env lean LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/LeastSquares/LSQRSolve.lean`, `lake build LeanFpAnalysis.FP.Algorithms.LeastSquares.LSQRSolve`, focused `#print axioms` checks for the six new public theorems, the touched-Lean-file placeholder scan, precise conflict-marker scan, `git diff --check`, and full `lake build` passed; the axiom checks report only `propext`, `Classical.choice`, and `Quot.sound`, and the full build reported only pre-existing QR/Givens/FastMatMul warnings.

Additional (20.18) increment: `lsScaledAugmentedMatrix_singularPair_plus_eigenpair`, `lsScaledAugmentedMatrix_singularPair_minus_eigenpair`, and `lsScaledAugmentedMatrix_leftNull_eigenpair` were added as packaged nonzero eigenpair certificates for the scaled augmented matrix.  The plus and minus branch theorems combine the existing singular-pair block-action identities with nonzero block-vector proofs under `sigma != 0` and `v != 0`; the left-null theorem combines the `alpha`-branch block action with nonzero support from a nonzero left-null vector.  This gives the later spectral multiplicity and condition-number bridge a cleaner source-facing eigenpair surface, but it does not prove global multiplicities, a spectral decomposition, or the matrix condition-number formula.  Validation: `lake env lean LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/LeastSquares/LSQRSolve.lean`, `lake build LeanFpAnalysis.FP.Algorithms.LeastSquares.LSQRSolve`, focused `#print axioms` checks for the three new public theorems, the touched-Lean-file placeholder scan, precise conflict-marker scan, `git diff --check`, and full `lake build` passed; the axiom checks report only `propext`, `Classical.choice`, and `Quot.sound`, and the full build reported only pre-existing QR/Givens/FastMatMul warnings.

Additional Theorem 20.9 associated-shape wrapper increment: `GQRAQTallAssocCase`, `GQRAQWideAssocCase`, `GQRAQTallAssocCase.mulVec_eq`, `GQRAQWideAssocCase.mulVec_eq`, `GeneralizedQRFactorization.exists_of_tall_qr_assoc_case`, and `GeneralizedQRFactorization.exists_of_wide_qr_assoc_case` were added to record and consume the associated-row/associated-column (20.28) shape hypotheses used by the construction route.  The wrappers reuse the existing supplied tall/wide QR packaging theorems, producing supplied (20.27) GQR data from exact `B^T` QR data and a supplied associated shape record.  This is exact supplied-shape algebra only: it does not construct `Q`, construct `U`, prove the shape records from actual factors, or prove computed GQR stability.  Validation: `lake env lean LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/LeastSquares/LSE.lean`, `lake build LeanFpAnalysis.FP.Algorithms.LeastSquares.LSE`, focused `#print axioms` checks for the four new public theorems, touched-file placeholder/conflict/trailing-whitespace scans, `git diff --check`, and full `lake build` passed; axiom checks report only `propext`, `Classical.choice`, and `Quot.sound`, and the full build reported only pre-existing QR/FastMatMul linter warnings.


Current exact-residual and zero-branch WKS branches: the source-residual hypothesis `lsResidualHigham A b y = 0` directly gives an exact least-squares minimizer, zero printed RHS, and `eta_F =` the printed (20.21) RHS.  For an exact minimizer with nonzero source residual, the residual annihilates the source block from the left, which now forces row-rank deficiency, `sigma_min = 0`, zero printed RHS, and `eta_F =` the printed RHS.  The wrapper `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorFormulaRHS_eq_zero_of_isLeastSquaresMinimizer` packages these branches, `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorFormulaRHS_eq_zero_of_etaF_eq_zero_of_positive_theta` adds the finite positive-`theta` zero-error-to-RHS-zero consistency direction, and `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorEtaF_eq_formulaRHS_of_etaF_eq_zero_of_positive_theta` packages the corresponding zero-error equality branch.  The zero-branch lower-bound wrappers now also prove `RHS <= eta_F` whenever the printed RHS is zero, in particular for exact minimizers and row-rank-deficient source blocks.  The converse-zero wrapper covers the nondegenerate case where the printed RHS is zero because `phi = 0`, forced by a positive row-side `sigma_min`; the non-minimizer classifiers prove that every remaining RHS-zero non-minimizer lies in the row-side `sigma_min = 0` branch, equivalently the row-rank-deficient branch; the global wrappers package these facts as zero iff `(exact minimizer or rowRank != m+1)` and positive iff `(not exact minimizer and rowRank = m+1)` for finite positive `theta` and nonzero `y`; the nondegenerate wrappers further show that under positive row-side `sigma_min`, and equivalently under full row rank of `[A phi(I-r r^+)]`, printed-RHS zero/positivity matches `eta_F` zero/positivity and a zero printed RHS gives the equality `eta_F = RHS`; the new left-panel theorem supplies that full source-block row rank whenever `A` itself has full row rank, and the full-left-panel wrappers use that theorem to make the printed-RHS zero/positivity and zero-equality consequences depend directly on full row rank of the original `A`; the eta/rank wrappers package zero iff `(eta_F=0 or rowRank != m+1)` and positive iff `(eta_F>0 and rowRank=m+1)`.  The remaining general WKS work is the positive nonzero-residual spectral lower-bound/attainment argument and the eigenvalue/limiting-case material.

Additional (20.20)-(20.21) zero-branch lower-bound increment: `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorFormulaRHS_le_etaF_of_formulaRHS_eq_zero`, `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorFormulaRHS_le_etaF_of_isLeastSquaresMinimizer`, and `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorFormulaRHS_le_etaF_of_formulaMatrixRowRank_ne_card` were added.  The first theorem proves the lower-bound side `RHS <= eta_F(y)` for every zero printed-RHS branch by combining `RHS = 0` with nonnegativity of `eta_F`; the next two package exact-minimizer and row-rank-deficient source-block branches through that fact.  This closes the lower-bound handoff on zero branches only; it does not prove the positive-branch WKS spectral lower bound, construct an attaining perturbation, derive the eigenvalue branch formula, or handle the general `theta = infinity` limiting theorem.  Validation: `lake env lean LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/LeastSquares/LSQRSolve.lean`, `lake build LeanFpAnalysis.FP.Algorithms.LeastSquares.LSQRSolve`, focused `#print axioms` checks for the three new public theorems, touched-Lean-file placeholder scan, anchored conflict-marker and trailing-whitespace scans, `git diff --check`, and full `lake build` passed; axiom checks report only `propext`, `Classical.choice`, and `Quot.sound`, and the full build reported only pre-existing QR/Givens/FastMatMul warnings.

Additional (20.20)-(20.21) nondegenerate zero-equality wrapper increment: `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorEtaF_eq_formulaRHS_of_formulaRHS_eq_zero_of_formulaMatrixRowRank_eq_card` and `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorEtaF_eq_formulaRHS_of_formulaRHS_eq_zero_of_left_panel_rowRank_eq_card` were added.  They convert the existing nondegenerate zero iff bridge into a direct equality statement: for positive finite `theta`, nonzero `y`, and a zero printed RHS, full row rank of `[A phi(I-r r^+)]` implies `eta_F(y) = RHS`; full row rank of the original data matrix `A` supplies that source-block rank condition automatically.  This is still only the zero branch and does not prove the positive-branch WKS lower-bound/attainment certificate, eigenvalue branch formula, or general `theta = infinity` limiting theorem.  Validation: `lake env lean LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/LeastSquares/LSQRSolve.lean`, `lake build LeanFpAnalysis.FP.Algorithms.LeastSquares.LSQRSolve`, focused `#print axioms` checks for the two new public theorems, touched-Lean-file placeholder scan, anchored conflict-marker and trailing-whitespace scans, `git diff --check`, and full `lake build` passed; axiom checks report only `propext`, `Classical.choice`, and `Quot.sound`, and the full build reported only pre-existing QR/Givens/FastMatMul warnings.

Additional (20.20)-(20.21) full-left-panel RHS/eta wrapper increment: `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorFormulaRHS_eq_zero_iff_etaF_eq_zero_of_theta_pos_of_y_ne_zero_of_left_panel_rowRank_eq_card` and `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorFormulaRHS_pos_iff_etaF_pos_of_theta_pos_of_y_ne_zero_of_left_panel_rowRank_eq_card` were added.  They combine the source-block full-row-rank inheritance theorem with the existing full-source-block nondegenerate WKS zero/positivity bridges: for positive finite `theta`, nonzero `y`, and full row rank of the original data matrix `A`, the printed RHS is zero iff `eta_F(y)=0`, and positive iff `eta_F(y)>0`.  This closes the nondegenerate row-rank side condition on the full-left-panel data-matrix branch, but it does not prove the WKS lower-bound/attainment certificate, eigenvalue branch formula, or general `theta = infinity` limiting theorem.  Validation: `lake env lean LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/LeastSquares/LSQRSolve.lean`, `lake build LeanFpAnalysis.FP.Algorithms.LeastSquares.LSQRSolve`, focused `#print axioms` checks for the two new public theorems, touched-Lean-file placeholder scan, anchored conflict-marker and trailing-whitespace scans, `git diff --check`, and full `lake build` passed; axiom checks report only `propext`, `Classical.choice`, and `Quot.sound`, and the full build reported only pre-existing QR/Givens/FastMatMul warnings.

Additional (20.20)-(20.21) full-left-panel row-rank increment: `lsRealRectRowRank_leftNull_eq_zero_of_rowRank_eq_card` and `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorFormulaMatrixRowRank_eq_card_of_left_panel_rowRank_eq_card` were added.  The first theorem packages the real row-rank/kernel consequence that full row rank rules out every real left-null vector.  The second applies a complexified rank-nullity bridge to the source block: if the original data matrix `A` has full row rank, then `[A phi(I-r r^+)]` has full row rank for every `theta`, residual vector `r`, and candidate `y`.  This closes the nondegenerate row-rank side condition on the full-left-panel branch, but it does not prove the full WKS lower-bound/attainment certificate, the eigenvalue branch formula, or the general `theta = infinity` limiting theorem.  Validation: `lake env lean LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/LeastSquares/LSQRSolve.lean`, `lake build LeanFpAnalysis.FP.Algorithms.LeastSquares.LSQRSolve`, focused `#print axioms` checks for the two new public theorems, touched-Lean-file placeholder scan, anchored conflict-marker and trailing-whitespace scans, `git diff --check`, and full `lake build` passed; axiom checks report only `propext`, `Classical.choice`, and `Quot.sound`, and the full build reported only pre-existing QR/Givens/FastMatMul warnings.

Additional (20.20)-(20.21) eta/rank branch-split increment: `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorFormulaRHS_eq_zero_iff_etaF_eq_zero_or_formulaMatrixRowRank_ne_card_of_theta_pos_of_y_ne_zero` and `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorFormulaRHS_pos_iff_etaF_pos_and_formulaMatrixRowRank_eq_card_of_theta_pos_of_y_ne_zero` were added.  They combine the finite positive-`theta` zero/positive `eta_F` characterizations with the source-facing row-rank branch decomposition: for nonzero `y`, the printed RHS is zero exactly when `eta_F(y)=0` or the source block `[A phi(I-r r^+)]` is row-rank deficient, and it is positive exactly when `eta_F(y)>0` and that source block has full row rank.  This is still branch classification only; it does not prove the WKS lower-bound/attainment certificate, the eigenvalue branch formula, or the general `theta = infinity` limiting theorem.  Validation: `lake env lean LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/LeastSquares/LSQRSolve.lean`, `lake build LeanFpAnalysis.FP.Algorithms.LeastSquares.LSQRSolve`, focused `#print axioms` checks for the two new public theorems, touched-Lean-file placeholder scan, anchored conflict-marker and trailing-whitespace scans, `git diff --check`, and full `lake build` passed; axiom checks report only `propext`, `Classical.choice`, and `Quot.sound`, and the full build reported only pre-existing QR/Givens/FastMatMul warnings.

Additional (20.20)-(20.21) row-rank branch-decomposition increment: `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorFormulaMatrixSigmaMin_eq_zero_iff_rowRank_ne_card`, `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorFormulaRHS_eq_zero_of_formulaMatrixRowRank_ne_card`, `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorFormulaRHS_eq_zero_iff_formulaMatrixRowRank_ne_card_of_not_isLeastSquaresMinimizer`, `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorFormulaRHS_pos_iff_formulaMatrixRowRank_eq_card_of_not_isLeastSquaresMinimizer`, `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorFormulaRHS_eq_zero_iff_isLeastSquaresMinimizer_or_formulaMatrixRowRank_ne_card_of_theta_pos_of_y_ne_zero`, and `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorFormulaRHS_pos_iff_not_isLeastSquaresMinimizer_and_formulaMatrixRowRank_eq_card_of_theta_pos_of_y_ne_zero` were added.  They recast the previous `sigma_min` zero/positive branch split using the source-facing row rank of `[A phi(I-r r^+)]`: row-rank deficiency forces the printed RHS to vanish, and for finite positive `theta` and nonzero `y`, positivity is exactly the non-minimizer plus full-row-rank branch.  This still does not prove the WKS lower-bound/attainment certificate, the eigenvalue branch formula, or the general `theta = infinity` limiting theorem.  Validation: `lake env lean LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/LeastSquares/LSQRSolve.lean`, `lake build LeanFpAnalysis.FP.Algorithms.LeastSquares.LSQRSolve`, focused `#print axioms` checks for the six new public theorems, touched-Lean-file placeholder scan, anchored conflict-marker and trailing-whitespace scans, `git diff --check`, and full `lake build` passed; axiom checks report only `propext`, `Classical.choice`, and `Quot.sound`, and the full build reported only pre-existing QR/Givens/FastMatMul warnings.

Additional (20.20)-(20.21) full-row-rank nondegenerate branch increment: `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorFormulaRHS_eq_zero_iff_etaF_eq_zero_of_theta_pos_of_y_ne_zero_of_formulaMatrixRowRank_eq_card` and `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorFormulaRHS_pos_iff_etaF_pos_of_theta_pos_of_y_ne_zero_of_formulaMatrixRowRank_eq_card` were added.  They use the row-rank/`sigma_min` equivalence for the source block `[A phi(I-r r^+)]` to restate the previous positive-row-`sigma_min` `eta_F`/RHS zero-positive bridge at the source-facing full-row-rank surface.  This still leaves the row-rank-deficient branch, the Walden--Karlson--Sun lower-bound/attainment certificate, the eigenvalue branch formula, and the general `theta = infinity` limiting theorem open.  Validation: `lake env lean LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/LeastSquares/LSQRSolve.lean`, `lake build LeanFpAnalysis.FP.Algorithms.LeastSquares.LSQRSolve`, focused `#print axioms` checks for the two new public theorems, touched-Lean-file placeholder scan, anchored conflict-marker and trailing-whitespace scans, `git diff --check`, and full `lake build` passed; axiom checks report only `propext`, `Classical.choice`, and `Quot.sound`, and the full build reported only pre-existing QR/Givens/FastMatMul warnings.

Additional (20.20)-(20.21) nondegenerate `eta_F`/RHS branch increment: `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorFormulaRHS_eq_zero_iff_etaF_eq_zero_of_theta_pos_of_y_ne_zero_of_sigmaMin_pos` and `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorFormulaRHS_pos_iff_etaF_pos_of_theta_pos_of_y_ne_zero_of_sigmaMin_pos` were added.  They combine the finite positive-`theta` zero/positive `eta_F` characterizations with the global printed-RHS branch decomposition to prove that, for nonzero `y` and positive row-side `sigma_min [A phi(I-r r^+)]`, RHS zero is equivalent to `eta_F(y)=0`, and RHS positivity is equivalent to `0 < eta_F(y)`.  This is only the nondegenerate branch bridge; the degenerate `sigma_min = 0` branch, the Walden--Karlson--Sun lower-bound/attainment certificate, the eigenvalue branch formula, and the general `theta = infinity` limiting theorem remain open.  Validation: `lake env lean LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/LeastSquares/LSQRSolve.lean`, `lake build LeanFpAnalysis.FP.Algorithms.LeastSquares.LSQRSolve`, focused `#print axioms` checks for the two new public theorems, touched-Lean-file placeholder/conflict/trailing-whitespace scans, `git diff --check`, and full `lake build` passed; axiom checks report only `propext`, `Classical.choice`, and `Quot.sound`, and the full build reported only pre-existing QR/Givens/FastMatMul warnings.

Additional (20.20)-(20.21) global branch-decomposition increment: `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorFormulaRHS_eq_zero_iff_isLeastSquaresMinimizer_or_sigmaMin_eq_zero_of_theta_pos_of_y_ne_zero` and `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorFormulaRHS_pos_iff_not_isLeastSquaresMinimizer_and_sigmaMin_pos_of_theta_pos_of_y_ne_zero` were added.  They combine the exact-minimizer zero-RHS theorem with the non-minimizer branch classifiers to prove, for finite positive `theta` and nonzero `y`, that the printed RHS is zero iff `y` is an exact least-squares minimizer or the row-side `sigma_min` branch is zero, and positive iff `y` is not a minimizer and the row-side `sigma_min` branch is positive.  This is a branch-decomposition wrapper only; it does not prove the Walden--Karlson--Sun lower-bound/attainment certificate, the eigenvalue branch formula, or the general `theta = infinity` limiting theorem.  Validation: `lake env lean LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/LeastSquares/LSQRSolve.lean`, `lake build LeanFpAnalysis.FP.Algorithms.LeastSquares.LSQRSolve`, focused `#print axioms` checks for the two new public theorems, touched-Lean-file placeholder/conflict/trailing-whitespace scans, `git diff --check`, and full `lake build` passed; axiom checks report only `propext`, `Classical.choice`, and `Quot.sound`, and the full build reported only pre-existing QR/Givens/FastMatMul warnings.

Additional (20.20)-(20.21) non-minimizer branch-classification increment: `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorPhi_pos_of_not_isLeastSquaresMinimizer_of_theta_pos_of_y_ne_zero`, `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorFormulaRHS_eq_zero_iff_sigmaMin_eq_zero_of_not_isLeastSquaresMinimizer`, and `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorFormulaRHS_pos_iff_sigmaMin_pos_of_not_isLeastSquaresMinimizer` were added.  They prove that, for finite positive `theta`, nonzero `y`, and a non-minimizer candidate, the printed scalar branch `phi` is strictly positive; hence RHS zero is equivalent to the degenerate row-side `sigma_min = 0` branch, and RHS positivity is equivalent to positive row-side `sigma_min`.  This narrows the remaining WKS formula work to the spectral lower/attainment certificate on the nonzero positive-branch case and the eigenvalue/limiting-case material.  Validation: `lake env lean LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/LeastSquares/LSQRSolve.lean`, `lake build LeanFpAnalysis.FP.Algorithms.LeastSquares.LSQRSolve`, focused `#print axioms` checks for the three new public theorems, touched-file placeholder/conflict/trailing-whitespace scans, `git diff --check`, and full `lake build` passed; axiom checks report only `propext`, `Classical.choice`, and `Quot.sound`, and the full build reported only pre-existing QR/Givens/FastMatMul warnings.

Additional (20.20)-(20.21) nondegenerate converse-zero increment: `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorMu_pos_of_theta_pos_of_y_ne_zero`, `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorPhi_eq_zero_iff_residual_eq_zero_of_theta_pos_of_y_ne_zero`, and `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorEtaF_eq_formulaRHS_of_formulaRHS_eq_zero_of_sigmaMin_pos` were added.  They prove that, for finite positive `theta` and nonzero `y`, the scalar `phi` is zero exactly when the source residual is zero; therefore a zero printed RHS with positive row-side `sigma_min [A phi(I-r r^+)]` collapses to the exact-residual branch and gives `eta_F(y) = RHS`.  This does not prove the degenerate `sigma_min = 0` converse branch, the general WKS lower-bound/attainment certificate, the eigenvalue branch formula, or the general `theta = infinity` limiting theorem.  Validation: `lake env lean LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/LeastSquares/LSQRSolve.lean`, `lake build LeanFpAnalysis.FP.Algorithms.LeastSquares.LSQRSolve`, focused `#print axioms` checks for the three new public theorems, touched-file placeholder/conflict/trailing-whitespace scans, `git diff --check`, and full `lake build` passed; axiom checks report only `propext`, `Classical.choice`, and `Quot.sound`, and the full build reported only pre-existing QR/Givens/FastMatMul warnings.

Additional (20.20)-(20.21) zero-error increment: `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorEtaF_eq_formulaRHS_of_etaF_eq_zero_of_positive_theta` was added as a finite positive-`theta` equality wrapper.  It derives exact least-squares minimizer status from `eta_F(y)=0`, then reuses the existing exact-minimizer WKS branch theorem to prove `eta_F(y)` equals the printed right-hand side.  This keeps the theorem at the zero-error branch surface and does not claim the open WKS spectral lower/attainment proof, eigenvalue formula, converse RHS-zero implication, or `theta = infinity` limiting theorem.

Additional (20.20)-(20.21) increment: `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorFormulaRHS_pos_iff` and `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorFormulaRHS_eq_zero_iff` were added as source-data branch-characterization wrappers for the printed RHS.  They substitute Higham's residual convention into the existing supplied-residual `min {phi, sigma_min}` positivity/zero theorems, proving exactly which displayed branch conditions make the source-data RHS positive or zero.  This supports the remaining WKS route by isolating the nonzero branch case, but it does not prove `RHS = 0 -> eta_F(y) = 0`, the general lower-bound/attainment certificate, the eigenvalue branch formula, or the `theta = infinity` limiting case.


Additional (20.18) spectral orthogonality increment: `isSymmetricFiniteMatrix_eigenvectors_sum_mul_eq_zero`, `lsScaledAugmentedMatrix_eigenvectors_sum_mul_eq_zero`, `lsScaledAugmentedMatrix_singularPair_plus_minus_normalized_dot_eq_zero`, `lsScaledAugmentedMatrix_singularPair_plus_leftNull_normalized_dot_eq_zero`, and `lsScaledAugmentedMatrix_singularPair_minus_leftNull_normalized_dot_eq_zero` were added.  The generic helper proves that distinct eigenvectors of any real symmetric finite matrix are Euclidean-orthogonal; the scaled-augmented wrapper applies it to `C(alpha)`; the three source-facing wrappers instantiate the printed normalized branches `[u; (sigma/lambda_+)v]`, `[u; (sigma/lambda_-)v]`, and `[w;0]` from (20.18).  This advances the spectral decomposition route for eqs. (20.18)-(20.19) and (20.18), but it does not prove a complete singular-vector/nullspace basis, global multiplicities, the full eigenvalue list, or the matrix condition-number bridge (20.19).

Additional (20.18)-(20.19) strict branch-monotonicity increment: `lsScaledAugmentedEigenvaluePlus_strictMono_sigma_nonneg` and `lsScaledAugmentedEigenvalueMinus_abs_strictMono_sigma_nonneg` were added.  They prove that, for nonnegative singular values, strict ordering of source singular values gives strict ordering of the positive branch and of the negative-branch magnitude.  This supplies the strict scalar separation needed by later extremal-branch and multiplicity arguments, but it does not prove the complete singular-vector/nullspace basis, global eigenvalue multiplicities, or the matrix condition-number bridge (20.19).  Validation: baseline and post-edit `lake env lean LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/LeastSquares/LSQRSolve.lean`, `lake build LeanFpAnalysis.FP.Algorithms.LeastSquares.LSQRSolve`, focused `#print axioms` checks for the two new public theorems, touched-Lean-file placeholder scan, anchored conflict-marker and trailing-whitespace scans, `git diff --check`, and full `lake build` passed; axiom checks report only `propext`, `Classical.choice`, and `Quot.sound`, and the full build reported only pre-existing Givens/FastMatMul warnings.

Additional (20.18)-(20.19) strict branch-ratio increment: `lsScaledAugmentedEigenvalue_branch_strict_extreme_bounds_of_sigma_bounds` and `lsScaledAugmentedEigenvalue_branch_ratio_lt_extreme_of_strict_sigma_bounds` were added.  They lift the strict scalar branch monotonicity facts to the source extremal interval: interior singular values have a strictly smaller positive branch than `sigmaMax`, a strictly larger negative-branch magnitude than `sigmaMin`, and consequently a strictly smaller branch ratio than the extremal branch ratio.  This supports the later matrix condition-number extremal-branch proof only; it does not prove the complete singular-vector/nullspace basis, global eigenvalue multiplicities, or the matrix condition-number bridge (20.19).  Validation: baseline and post-edit `lake env lean LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/LeastSquares/LSQRSolve.lean`, `lake build LeanFpAnalysis.FP.Algorithms.LeastSquares.LSQRSolve`, focused `#print axioms` checks for the two new public theorems, touched-Lean-file placeholder scan, anchored conflict-marker and trailing-whitespace scans, `git diff --check`, and full `lake build` passed; axiom checks report only `propext`, `Classical.choice`, and `Quot.sound`, and the full build reported only pre-existing Givens/FastMatMul warnings.

Additional (20.18) normalized branch dot-product increment: `finAppend_sum_mul_eq`, `finAppend_sum_mul_smul_eq`, `lsScaledAugmentedMatrix_singularPair_normalized_dot_eq`, and `lsScaledAugmentedMatrix_singularPair_normalized_dot_eq_zero_of_orthogonal` were added.  They split the finite dot product of appended branch vectors `[u; beta v]` and `[w; gamma z]` into the left-block dot product plus `beta * gamma` times the right-block dot product, and package the zero result from supplied component orthogonality.  This supports the later complete orthogonal singular-vector/nullspace basis route only; it does not prove singular-vector basis existence, global eigenvalue multiplicities, or the matrix condition-number bridge (20.19). Additional (20.18) same-eigenspace branch-orthogonality increment: `lsScaledAugmentedMatrix_singularPair_plus_plus_normalized_dot_eq`, `lsScaledAugmentedMatrix_singularPair_plus_minus_normalized_dot_eq`, `lsScaledAugmentedMatrix_singularPair_minus_minus_normalized_dot_eq`, their component-orthogonality corollaries, `lsScaledAugmentedMatrix_singularPair_plus_leftNull_normalized_dot_eq`, `lsScaledAugmentedMatrix_singularPair_minus_leftNull_normalized_dot_eq`, their left-orthogonality corollaries, `lsScaledAugmentedMatrix_leftNull_leftNull_dot_eq`, and `lsScaledAugmentedMatrix_leftNull_leftNull_dot_eq_zero_of_left_orthogonal` were added.  They specialize the normalized appended dot-product expansion to plus/plus, plus/minus, minus/minus, branch/left-null, and left-null/left-null source shapes and prove zero corollaries from supplied component orthogonality.  This supports repeated singular-value and `alpha` left-null eigenspace orthogonality only; it does not prove component singular-vector/nullspace basis existence, global multiplicities, or the matrix condition-number bridge (20.19). Additional (20.18) normalized branch squared-norm increment: `lsScaledAugmentedMatrix_singularPair_normalized_vecNorm2Sq_eq`, `lsScaledAugmentedMatrix_singularPair_plus_normalized_vecNorm2Sq_eq`, `lsScaledAugmentedMatrix_singularPair_minus_normalized_vecNorm2Sq_eq`, `lsScaledAugmentedMatrix_singularPair_plus_normalized_vecNorm2Sq_eq_one_add_sq_of_unit_components`, `lsScaledAugmentedMatrix_singularPair_minus_normalized_vecNorm2Sq_eq_one_add_sq_of_unit_components`, `lsScaledAugmentedMatrix_leftNull_vecNorm2Sq_eq`, and `lsScaledAugmentedMatrix_leftNull_vecNorm2Sq_eq_one_of_unit_component` were added.  They reduce the squared Euclidean norm of `[u; beta v]`, the printed plus/minus branch vectors, and the left-null branch `[u;0]` to supplied component squared norms; the unit-component corollaries give the expected `1 + (sigma/lambda_\pm)^2` and `1` formulas. Additional Theorem 20.3 source-facing compact-budget wrapper increment: `theorem20_3_householder_qr_ls_backward_error_compactBudget_of_diagDominant_storedLower_activePivot_actualUnitRoundoff_horizonBudget` was added.  It packages the stored-lower active-pivot actual-unit horizon-budget exact-minimizer theorem under a Theorem 20.3 name, proving that the computed `fl_backSub` vector is an exact least-squares minimizer for perturbed data with compact-sequence Frobenius/RHS budgets while deriving source-denominator nonbreakdown and global stage-budget monotonicity internally.  This remains a conditional endgame step only: diagonal dominance, active-pivot selection, stage-budget comparison, and finite-max smallness hypotheses remain visible.  Validation started with baseline and post-edit `lake env lean LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/LeastSquares/LSQRSolve.lean`; full milestone validation is recorded for this commit.

Additional Theorem 20.3 row-max/dual-budget compact-budget wrapper increment: `theorem20_3_householder_qr_ls_backward_error_compactBudget_of_activePivot_rowMax_dualBudget_actualUnitRoundoff` was added and then tightened to consume the scalar stage-budget/row-max comparison defect directly.  It packages the actual-unit active-pivot row-max/dual-budget exact-minimizer theorem under a source-facing Theorem 20.3 name, proving that the computed `fl_backSub` vector is an exact least-squares minimizer for perturbed data with compact-sequence Frobenius/RHS budgets.  This is still conditional, but it removes the local diagonal-dominance, canonical finite-max-smallness, and pointwise stage-vs-row-max hypotheses from this named wrapper; determinant/conditioning, dual compact-budget, row-max defect, scalar comparison defect, active-pivot, and compact global-product hypotheses remain visible.  Validation: `lake env lean LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/LeastSquares/LSQRSolve.lean`, `git diff --check -- LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/LeastSquares/LSQRSolve.lean`, and full post-merge `lake build` passed; documentation validation is recorded in the follow-up ledger commit.

Additional Theorem 20.3 stage-diagonal/dual-budget compact-budget wrapper increment: `theorem20_3_householder_qr_ls_backward_error_compactBudget_of_activePivot_stageDiag_dualBudget_actualUnitRoundoff` was added with exact-minimizer bridge theorems `exists_perturbed_ls_minimizer_of_stored_trailing_householder_sequence_topBlock_fl_backSub_gamma_bound_explicitCompactBudget_of_signed_alpha_leadingBlock_det_ne_zero_kappaInf_selfNorm_dualBudget_signedStageUniformBudget_globalCompactBudget_activeMaxPivot_completedColumns_globalProduct_stageDiagDefect_offdiag_rows` and its actual-unit-roundoff specialization.  This is the current strongest active-pivot Theorem 20.3 wrapper: it removes the row-max diagonal defect and scalar comparison defect from the public surface and replaces them with the single finite scalar `storedQRStageDiagLowerDefectBudget <= 0`.  It remains conditional on determinant/conditioning data, dual compact-budget data, active-pivot selection, monotone global stage budget, stored-loop recurrence, signed `alpha`, and compact global-product smallness.  Validation: `lake env lean LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/LeastSquares/LSQRSolve.lean`, `git diff --check -- LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/LeastSquares/LSQRSolve.lean`, merge with latest `origin/main`, full post-merge `lake build`, and push to branch and `main` passed.

Additional Theorem 20.3 row-budget-control compact-budget wrapper increment: `theorem20_3_householder_qr_ls_backward_error_compactBudget_of_rowBudgetControl_dualBudget_actualUnitRoundoff` was added with exact-minimizer bridge theorem `exists_perturbed_ls_minimizer_of_stored_trailing_householder_sequence_topBlock_fl_backSub_gamma_bound_explicitCompactBudget_of_signed_alpha_leadingBlock_det_ne_zero_kappaInf_selfNorm_dualBudget_rowBudgetControl_globalProduct_of_actualUnitRoundoff_no_gammaValid`.  This packages the existing row-budget-control global-product route under a source-facing Theorem 20.3 name with actual unit roundoff.  It removes the active-pivot, monotone stage-budget, and stage-diagonal-defect fields from this public surface by requiring the single packaged `StoredQRDisplayedRowBudgetControl` certificate instead.  It remains conditional on determinant/conditioning data, dual compact-budget data, the displayed row-budget certificate, and compact global-product smallness.  Validation: `lake env lean LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/LeastSquares/LSQRSolve.lean`, `git diff --check -- LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/LeastSquares/LSQRSolve.lean`, merge with latest `origin/main`, and push to branch and `main` passed.

Additional (20.24) LSE uniqueness bridge increment: `exists_unique_isLSEMinimizer_iff_nullIntersectionTrivial_of_exists` was added.  It proves that, once an exact equality-constrained least-squares minimizer exists, existence-and-uniqueness of that minimizer is equivalent to the null-intersection condition in (20.24).  The forward direction shifts a minimizer by a common null vector and uses objective invariance; the reverse direction reuses `IsLSEMinimizer.eq_of_nullIntersectionTrivial`.  This is exact LSE algebra only: it does not prove full-row-rank minimizer existence, GQR factor construction, associated (20.28) shape construction, or computed GQR stability.  Validation started with baseline and post-edit `lake env lean LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/LeastSquares/LSE.lean`; full milestone validation is recorded for this commit.

Additional (20.24) stacked full-column-rank uniqueness bridge increment: `exists_unique_isLSEMinimizer_iff_lseStackedFullColumnRank_of_exists` was added.  It restates the existing-minimizer uniqueness iff in the source wording that `[A^T,B^T]^T` has full column rank, represented locally by injectivity of the vertical stack `[A;B]`.  This is an exact corollary of `exists_unique_isLSEMinimizer_iff_nullIntersectionTrivial_of_exists` and `LSENullIntersectionTrivial.iff_lseStackedFullColumnRank`; it does not prove numeric rank equivalence, full-row-rank minimizer existence, GQR construction, associated (20.28) shape construction, or computed GQR stability.  Validation started with baseline and post-edit `lake env lean LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/LeastSquares/LSE.lean`; full milestone validation is recorded for this commit.

Additional (20.24)/(20.27) source stacked-rank GQR handoff increment: `IsLSEMinimizer.eq_of_lseStackedFullColumnRank` and `GeneralizedQRFactorization.exists_unique_lse_minimizer_of_fullRowRank_stackedFullColumnRank` were added.  The first theorem gives direct uniqueness of any two exact LSE minimizers from the local stacked full-column-rank condition; the second composes that source-rank wording with the supplied-GQR exact minimizer theorem, using full-row-rank consistency and stacked full-column rank at the public surface.  This is exact supplied-factor algebra only: it does not prove numeric rank equivalence, construct the GQR factors, derive associated (20.28) shapes from actual QR factors, or prove computed GQR stability.  Validation started with baseline and post-edit `lake env lean LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/LeastSquares/LSE.lean`; full milestone validation is recorded for this commit.

Additional (20.20)-(20.21) nondegenerate branchwise certificate increment: `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorEtaF_eq_formulaRHS_of_formulaMatrixRowRank_eq_card_of_positive_formula_certificate` and `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorEtaF_eq_formulaRHS_of_left_panel_rowRank_eq_card_of_positive_formula_certificate` were added.  They package the finite-positive-`theta` nondegenerate WKS branch so that full row rank of `[A phi(I-r r^+)]` (or full row rank of the original `A` panel) plus `y ≠ 0` is enough to split the printed RHS into two cases: the existing zero-RHS branch proves `eta_F(y) = RHS` automatically, and lower-bound/attainment certificates are required only on the positive-RHS branch.  This narrows the remaining WKS work without hiding it: the positive-branch spectral lower-bound proof, attaining perturbation construction, eigenvalue branch formula, degenerate source-block branch, and `theta = infinity` limiting theorem remain open. Additional (20.20)-(20.21) branch-eliminated degenerate-attainer increment: `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorEtaF_eq_formulaRHS_of_formulaMatrixRowRank_eq_card_of_formulaMatrixSigmaMin_attainer_projected_eq_zero_of_dot_b_ne_zero` and `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorEtaF_eq_formulaRHS_of_left_panel_rowRank_eq_card_of_formulaMatrixSigmaMin_attainer_projected_eq_zero_of_dot_b_ne_zero` were added.  They combine the zero-RHS equality branch, the scalar `phi` upper branch, and the degenerate row-side `sigma_min` attainer branch into one source-block full-row-rank theorem, plus a full-left-panel wrapper.  This removes the explicit outer-minimum branch-selection hypothesis from the degenerate attainer route, but it still leaves the projected-attainer/source-dot conditions, the genuine nondegenerate source-block construction, the eigenvalue branch formula, and `theta = infinity` open.
