Additional (20.18)-(20.19) inverse-norm branch-ratio increment: `finiteOpNorm2Le_inverse_abs_recip_eigenvalue_le_of_isLeftInverse`, `lsScaledAugmentedMatrix_singularPair_plus_abs_recip_eigenvalue_le_of_inverse_finiteOpNorm2Le`, `lsScaledAugmentedMatrix_singularPair_minus_abs_recip_eigenvalue_le_of_inverse_finiteOpNorm2Le`, and `lsScaledAugmentedMatrix_singularPair_plus_minus_abs_ratio_le_opNorm_mul_inverseOpNorm` were added.  The generic theorem proves that a finite operator-2 bound for a left-inverse candidate dominates the reciprocal magnitude of a witnessed nonzero eigenvalue; the Chapter 20 wrappers instantiate the result for the plus and minus singular-pair branches of the scaled augmented matrix `C(alpha)` and combine the direct and inverse bounds into the product estimate `|lambda_+| / |lambda_-| <= L * D`.  This advances the condition-number side of (20.19) without hiding the remaining work: it still does not construct the full singular-vector/nullspace eigenbasis, prove global multiplicities, prove the selected branches are the extremal eigenvalues, construct or identify the inverse of `C(alpha)`, or close the full matrix condition-number formula.  Validation: `lake env lean LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Analysis/MatrixAlgebra.lean`, `lake env lean LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/LeastSquares/LSQRSolve.lean`, `lake build LeanFpAnalysis.FP.Analysis.MatrixAlgebra`, `lake build LeanFpAnalysis.FP.Algorithms.LeastSquares.LSQRSolve`, focused `#print axioms` checks for the four new public theorems, touched-Lean-file placeholder scan, anchored conflict-marker and trailing-whitespace scans, `git diff --check`, and full `lake build` passed; axiom checks report only `propext`, `Classical.choice`, and `Quot.sound`, and the full build reported only pre-existing Givens/FastMatMul warnings.

Additional (20.18)-(20.19) `kappa2` condition-number-surface increment: `lsScaledAugmentedMatrix_singularPair_plus_minus_abs_ratio_le_kappa2` and `lsScaledAugmentedMatrix_singularPair_balanced_sigma_ratio_le_kappa2_of_alpha_eq_div_sqrt_two` were added.  The first theorem specializes the finite-bound product bridge to the repository's exact `kappa2` definition using `opNorm2 C(alpha)` and `opNorm2 Cinv`; the second combines that source-facing condition-number bridge with the balanced-scaling scalar lower bound, proving `sqrt 2 * (sigmaMax / sigmaMin) <= kappa2 C(alpha) Cinv` from supplied extremal singular-pair witnesses, `sigmaMin > 0`, `sigmaMin <= sigmaMax`, `alpha = sigmaMin / sqrt 2`, and an explicit inverse candidate.  This makes the lower half of the (20.19) balanced-scaling route available at the condition-number surface, but it still does not construct the complete eigenbasis, prove global multiplicities, prove extremality of the supplied singular pairs, construct or identify the inverse of `C(alpha)`, or close the full formula.  Validation: `lake env lean LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/LeastSquares/LSQRSolve.lean`, `lake build LeanFpAnalysis.FP.Algorithms.LeastSquares.LSQRSolve`, focused `#print axioms` checks for both public theorems, touched-file placeholder/conflict/trailing-whitespace scans, `git diff --check`, and full `lake build` passed; axiom checks report only `propext`, `Classical.choice`, and `Quot.sound`, and the full build reported only pre-existing Givens/FastMatMul warnings.

Additional (20.18)-(20.19) reciprocal-diagonal inverse-candidate increment: `finiteMatMul_assoc`, `finiteMatMul_finiteIdMatrix_left`, `finiteMatMul_finiteIdMatrix_right`, `finiteMatMul_finiteDiagonal`, `finiteMatMul_finiteDiagonal_inv_self`, `finiteMatMul_finiteDiagonal_self_inv`, `finiteMatMul_matTranspose_self_of_isOrthogonal`, `finiteMatMul_self_matTranspose_of_isOrthogonal`, `isInverse_of_isOrthogonal_diagonalization`, and `kappa2_le_mul_of_isOrthogonal_diagonalization_inverse_candidate` were added, together with the source-facing wrappers `lsScaledAugmentedMatrix_isInverse_of_orthogonal_diagonalization`, `lsScaledAugmentedMatrix_kappa2_le_mul_of_orthogonal_diagonalization_inverse_candidate`, and `lsScaledAugmentedMatrix_kappa2_le_two_sigma_ratio_of_balanced_orthogonal_diagonalization_inverse_candidate`.  These prove that, from a supplied complete orthogonal diagonalization of `C(alpha)` with nonzero diagonal entries, the reciprocal diagonal in the same orthogonal basis is a two-sided inverse and satisfies the balanced (20.19) upper `kappa2` certificate under the existing branch magnitude bounds.  This closes the algebraic inverse-candidate handoff but still does not construct the complete singular-vector/nullspace eigenbasis, prove global multiplicities, prove the printed (20.18) branch list is exhaustive, derive diagonal nonzero certificates from branch formulas, or close the full condition-number formula. Additional (20.18)-(20.19) branch-classified diagonal certificate increment: `lsScaledAugmentedDiagonalBranch_abs_min_le_of_alpha_eq_div_sqrt_two`, `lsScaledAugmentedDiagonalBranch_ne_zero_of_alpha_eq_div_sqrt_two`, and `lsScaledAugmentedDiagonalBranch_recip_abs_le_of_alpha_eq_div_sqrt_two` were added.  These prove that, under the balanced scaling `alpha = sigmaMin / sqrt 2`, a supplied diagonal list whose entries are classified as the left-nullspace branch `alpha` or printed plus/minus singular-value branches over `[sigmaMin, sigmaMax]` has every entry bounded below in magnitude by `|lambda_-(sigmaMin)|`, every entry nonzero, and every reciprocal magnitude bounded by `|lambda_-(sigmaMin)|^{-1}`. Additional Theorem 20.9 orthogonal-completion increment: `exists_orthogonal_completion_tall_qr_block` was added.  From a rectangular exact factorization `B^T = Q1 R` whose columns are orthonormal, it constructs a square orthogonal completion `Q` that preserves the first `p` columns and proves the transformed tall block identity `Q^T B^T = [R;0]`.  This is the first construction step on the GQR existence route that removes a previously supplied square `Q`; it still assumes the rectangular exact QR factorization data, does not construct the `U` factor for `A Q`, does not prove tall/wide associated shape records, and does not close the full Theorem 20.9 existence/reconstruction statement.  The GPT Pro oracle was consulted for route selection but produced no adopted proof content; the local route came from repository search for orthonormal-completion infrastructure.  Validation: `lake env lean LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/LeastSquares/LSE.lean`, `lake build LeanFpAnalysis.FP.Algorithms.LeastSquares.LSE`, `git diff --check`, and full `lake build` passed; after the linter cleanup, the focused LSE check reports no new local `simpa` warnings, and the full build reports only pre-existing GivensSpec/FastMatMul warnings.

Additional Theorem 20.9 MGS-to-constraint-block increment: `exists_transpose_tall_qr_of_mgs_orthonormal` and `exists_gqr_constraint_block_of_mgs_orthonormal` were added.  They instantiate the new orthogonal-completion theorem with the repository's exact MGS factorization of `B^T`, carry the constructed square orthogonal `Q` through `Q^T B^T = [R;0]`, and expose the GQR constraint block `B Q = [S 0]` with `S` lower triangular.  This removes the arbitrary rectangular factorization and upper-triangular `R` hypotheses from the `B^T` side, but still explicitly assumes the missing theorem that the MGS `Q` columns are orthonormal under the nonzero-stage hypotheses; it also does not construct the `U` factor for `A Q`, associated tall/wide shape records, or the full Theorem 20.9 reconstruction.  Validation: `lake env lean LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/LeastSquares/LSE.lean` and full `lake build` passed after merging latest `origin/main`; the full build reported only pre-existing GivensSpec/FastMatMul warnings.

Additional Theorem 20.9 MGS orthogonality atom increment: `gsDot_self_eq_finiteVecNorm2Sq`, `gsDot_projectAway_self`, `gsDot_projectAway_eq_zero_of_unit`, and `modifiedGramSchmidtQ_dot_vectors_succ_later_eq_zero` were added in the QR Gram-Schmidt layer.  The source-facing step theorem proves that after exact MGS step `k`, each later residual column is orthogonal to the current normalized `q_k` column.  This is a direct dependency for proving `GramSchmidtOrthonormalColumns (modifiedGramSchmidtQ B^T)`, but it does not yet prove persistence through later steps or the full pairwise orthonormality induction.  Validation: `lake env lean LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/QR/GramSchmidt.lean`, `lake build LeanFpAnalysis.FP.Algorithms.Underdetermined.UnderdeterminedSolve`, and full `lake build` passed after merging latest `origin/main`; the full build reported only pre-existing GivensSpec/FastMatMul warnings.

Additional Theorem 20.9 MGS orthogonality-persistence increment: `gsDot_projectAway_left` and `gsDot_projectAway_eq_zero_of_left_orthogonal` were added.  They prove that projecting a residual away from a new direction preserves an older dot-zero relation whenever the old direction is orthogonal to the new direction.  This is the second local algebra atom for the full MGS orthonormality induction; it still does not assemble the induction over stages/column pairs.  Validation: `lake env lean LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/QR/GramSchmidt.lean` and full `lake build` passed; the full build reported only pre-existing GivensSpec/FastMatMul warnings.

Additional Theorem 20.9 MGS conditional orthogonality bridge increment: `gsDot_normalize_right`, `modifiedGramSchmidtQ_dot_vectors_stage_eq_zero_of_lt_of_prev`, and `modifiedGramSchmidtQ_dot_eq_zero_of_lt_of_prev` were added.  The new stage theorem carries an older `q_i` residual orthogonality relation from stage `i+1` through stage `j` assuming the intervening normalized columns are already orthogonal to `q_i`, and the pair theorem transfers that residual result to the final normalized `q_j`.  This is the induction bridge for the full MGS orthonormal-columns route; it still leaves the final unconditional strong induction over column pairs and the downstream `U`/associated-shape GQR construction open.  Validation: `lake env lean LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/QR/GramSchmidt.lean` and `git diff --check -- LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/QR/GramSchmidt.lean` passed after merging latest `origin/main`; no oracle consultation was used for this update.

Additional Theorem 20.9 MGS pairwise orthogonality increment: `modifiedGramSchmidtQ_dot_eq_zero_of_lt` was added.  Under the standard nonzero-stage MGS hypothesis, it proves every earlier normalized column `q_i` is orthogonal to every later normalized column `q_j` by strong induction over the later column and the conditional stage bridge above.  This closes the off-diagonal MGS orthogonality dependency; the next remaining MGS package step is turning unit column norms plus pairwise dot-zero into `GramSchmidtOrthonormalColumns (modifiedGramSchmidtQ A)`.  Validation: `lake env lean LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/QR/GramSchmidt.lean` and `git diff --check -- LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/QR/GramSchmidt.lean` passed after merging latest `origin/main`; no oracle consultation was used for this update.

Additional Theorem 20.9 MGS orthonormal-columns package increment: `gsDot_comm`, `rectangularGram_eq_gsDot`, and `modifiedGramSchmidtQ_orthonormal_columns` were added.  The package theorem combines the already proved unit column norm result and pairwise dot-zero theorem to prove `GramSchmidtOrthonormalColumns (modifiedGramSchmidtQ A)` from the standard nonzero-stage MGS hypothesis.  This closes the QR-side orthonormality dependency for the exact `B^T` MGS factorization route; the next Theorem 20.9 work is to use it to remove the orthonormality assumption from the GQR constraint-block construction and then build the `U`/associated-shape side.  Validation: `lake env lean LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/QR/GramSchmidt.lean` and `git diff --check -- LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/QR/GramSchmidt.lean` passed after merging latest `origin/main`; no oracle consultation was used for this update.

Additional Theorem 20.9 MGS-to-GQR constraint-block increment: `exists_transpose_tall_qr_of_mgs` and `exists_gqr_constraint_block_of_mgs` were added.  These wrappers use `modifiedGramSchmidtQ_orthonormal_columns` to remove the explicit orthonormality hypothesis from the previous exact MGS `B^T` QR and GQR constraint-block construction, so only the nonzero MGS stage-normalizer hypothesis remains on the QR side.  This closes the `B Q = [S 0]` construction route under exact MGS data; it still does not construct the `U` factor for `A Q`, prove the associated tall/wide shape records, or package the full Theorem 20.9 reconstruction.  Validation: `lake build LeanFpAnalysis.FP.Algorithms.QR.GramSchmidt`, `lake env lean LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/LeastSquares/LSE.lean`, and `git diff --check -- LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/LeastSquares/LSE.lean` passed after merging latest `origin/main`; no oracle consultation was used for this update.

Additional Theorem 20.9 MGS constraint plus associated-shape composition increment: `GeneralizedQRFactorization.exists_of_tall_mgs_constraint_and_assoc_shape` and `GeneralizedQRFactorization.exists_of_wide_mgs_constraint_and_assoc_shape` were added.  These theorems consume exact MGS nonzero-stage data for `B^T`, construct the GQR constraint side internally, and package a full `GeneralizedQRFactorization` whenever the remaining `A Q` side supplies an associated tall or wide `U^T A Q` shape for the completed `Q`.  This isolates the remaining constructive obligation to the `U`/associated-shape side; it still does not prove that obligation or the final source rank/nonsingularity equivalence.  Validation: `lake env lean LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/LeastSquares/LSE.lean`, `git diff --check -- LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/LeastSquares/LSE.lean`, merge with latest `origin/main`, and full `lake build` passed; the full build reported only pre-existing `GivensSpec`/`FastMatMul` warnings.  No oracle consultation was used for this update.

Additional Theorem 20.9 wide associated-shape construction increment: `gqrReverseSquare_lowerTriangular_of_upper`, `finPermMatrix_orthogonal`, `gqrReverseRowsOfQRReversedCols`, `exists_orthogonal_gqrReverseSquare_of_qr_reversed_cols`, `gqrAQWideAssocX`, `gqrAQWideAssocL`, `gqrAQWideBlockAssoc_extract_eq`, `gqrAQWideAssocL_matMulRectLeft`, `GQRAQWideAssocCase.of_trailing_lower`, and `GQRAQWideAssocCase.exists_of_trailing_qr_reversed_cols` were added.  These declarations make the wide (20.28) `U^T A Q = [X L]` shape constructive from a supplied QR transform of the column-reversed trailing square block: the QR upper triangular block is converted into a lower triangular block by reversing rows and columns, and the resulting orthogonal left factor supplies a `Nonempty GQRAQWideAssocCase`.  This removes the completely opaque wide associated-shape assumption for that supplied trailing-block QR route, but it still does not instantiate that route for the actual `A Q` selected by the MGS `B^T` construction, does not construct the tall associated shape, and does not close the full Theorem 20.9 reconstruction.  Validation: `lake env lean LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/LeastSquares/LSE.lean` passed; the milestone was then committed, merged with latest `origin/main`, and pushed to both the working branch and `main`.  No oracle consultation was used for this update.

Additional Theorem 20.9 wide associated-shape exact-MGS increment: `lsQRTallBlock_zero`, `exists_orthogonal_gqrReverseSquare_of_mgs_reversed_cols`, and `GQRAQWideAssocCase.exists_of_trailing_mgs_reversed_cols` were added.  These instantiate the supplied trailing-block QR route with the repository's exact modified Gram-Schmidt factorization of the column-reversed trailing square block, under the standard nonzero-stage MGS hypotheses, and return the orthogonal `U` plus a `Nonempty GQRAQWideAssocCase` for the transformed wide block.  This closes the wide trailing-block QR-to-shape route under exact MGS data, but it still does not construct the tall associated shape, package the completed `B^T` MGS constraint side with this `A Q` construction into the full source reconstruction theorem, prove numeric rank equivalences, or prove computed GQR stability. Additional Theorem 20.9 rank-to-MGS nonbreakdown base increment: `rectMatMulVec_finiteBasisVec_gsColumn` and `modifiedGramSchmidtVectors_zero_norm_ne_zero_of_rectMatMulVec_injective` were added in the Gram-Schmidt layer.  The new dependency proves the base case of the rank-to-MGS route: an injective rectangular column map rules out a zero input column and therefore rules out a zero stage-0 MGS normalizer.  This starts discharging the explicit MGS nonzero-stage hypotheses used by the exact GQR construction, but it does not yet prove the full induction over later MGS residual stages or specialize full row rank of `B` to injectivity of `B^T`. It proves that the local full-row-rank condition for `B` makes the transpose column map `B^T` injective, closing the source-rank-to-rectangular-injectivity bridge needed by the exact-MGS route. Additional Theorem 20.9 all-stage rank-to-MGS nonbreakdown increment: `modifiedGramSchmidtVectors_exists_source_coeffs` and `modifiedGramSchmidtVectors_norm_ne_zero_of_rectMatMulVec_injective` were added in the QR Gram-Schmidt layer.  The first theorem proves that every exact MGS residual is a linear combination of the original columns with coefficient `1` on its own source column and zero coefficients on later source columns; the second uses that invariant to show an injective rectangular column map rules out zero active residuals at every MGS stage.  This closes the later-stage nonbreakdown induction that was still open after the stage-0 bridge, but it does not by itself construct the tall associated shape, prove numeric rank equivalence, or prove computed GQR stability. Additional Theorem 20.9 full-row-rank exact-MGS tall-route increment: `GeneralizedQRFactorization.exists_of_tall_fullRowRank_constraint_and_assoc_shape`, `GeneralizedQRFactorization.exists_unique_lse_minimizer_of_tall_mgs_constraint_and_assoc_shape`, `GeneralizedQRFactorization.exists_unique_lse_minimizer_of_tall_fullRowRank_constraint_and_assoc_shape`, `GeneralizedQRFactorization.exists_unique_method_solution_of_tall_mgs_constraint_and_assoc_shape`, and `GeneralizedQRFactorization.exists_unique_method_solution_of_tall_fullRowRank_constraint_and_assoc_shape` were added.  These bring the tall branch to the same B-side rank surface as the wide branch: full row rank of `B` now discharges the exact-MGS nonzero-stage hypotheses for the `B^T` constraint side, while the remaining tall `A Q` obligation is the associated-row `[0; L]` shape construction.  The public method theorem returns constructed GQR data, unique exact triangular solve coordinates, and the unique exact LSE minimizer under source full-row-rank and stacked-full-column-rank assumptions plus that remaining associated-shape construction. GPT Pro oracle consultation log, Theorem 20.9 GQR A-side route: consulted after local work showed that deriving MGS nonbreakdown for the full wide trailing `gqrAQWideAssocL (A Q)` block from the source rank assumptions is likely too strong.  Prompt summary: asked for a source-faithful construction of the `U^T A Q` associated shape without assuming nonbreakdown for a possibly rank-deficient block, comparing rank-tolerant triangularization, smaller full-rank subblock QR, and block/kernel arguments.  Answer summary: recommended proving the kernel consequence `A` injective on `ker B`, identifying `range Q2 = ker B` after `B Q = [S 0]` with `S` nonsingular, deriving injectivity of `A Q2`, and applying QR/MGS only to that safe `Q2` block before extending to a full orthogonal `U`; it explicitly warned against QR/MGS on the full wide trailing block.  Adopted status: partially adopted in Lean via the supplied-GQR `GeneralizedQRFactorization.A_Q2_*` bridges, the construction-level `gqrAQ2_*_of_constraint_block_nullIntersection` bridges, `exists_gqr_constraint_block_and_A_Q2_mgs_of_fullRowRank_stackedFullColumnRank`, `exists_gqr_constraint_block_and_A_Q2_mgs_qr_of_fullRowRank_stackedFullColumnRank`, and the column-reversed smaller-block QR input `exists_gqr_constraint_block_and_reversed_A_Q2_mgs_qr_of_fullRowRank_stackedFullColumnRank`; the next local target is to use this explicit reversed smaller-block QR witness to build the associated (20.28) shape without assuming nonbreakdown for the whole trailing block.  Verification result: focused `lake env lean LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/LeastSquares/LSE.lean` checks passed for the Lean milestones; after merging collaborator changes from `origin/main`, full `lake build` also passed at the pushed merged head with only pre-existing QR/Givens/FastMatMul warnings.

Additional Theorem 20.9 Q2-kernel increment: `GeneralizedQRFactorization.A_Q2_kernel_trivial_of_nullIntersectionTrivial` proves the oracle-recommended kernel bridge at the supplied-GQR surface: if `A (Q [0; y2]) = 0`, then `Q [0; y2]` also satisfies the `B` constraint block equation, so the local null-intersection condition forces `y2 = 0`.  `GeneralizedQRFactorization.A_Q2_kernel_trivial_of_stackedFullColumnRank` exposes the same result under the source stacked-full-column-rank hypothesis by reusing `LSENullIntersectionTrivial.iff_lseStackedFullColumnRank`.  This is still supplied-GQR block algebra: it does not construct `Q`, construct `U`, prove the associated (20.28) shape, or prove computed GQR stability.  Validation: `lake env lean LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/LeastSquares/LSE.lean`, `git diff --check -- LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/LeastSquares/LSE.lean`, merge with latest `origin/main`, and push to branch and `main` passed.

Additional Theorem 20.9 AQ2 MGS increment: `gqrAQ2Block` and `gqrAQ2Block_mulVec` name the smaller trailing source block `A Q2` and its vector action.  `GeneralizedQRFactorization.A_Q2_rectMatMulVec_injective_of_nullIntersectionTrivial` and `GeneralizedQRFactorization.A_Q2_rectMatMulVec_injective_of_stackedFullColumnRank` convert the Q2 kernel bridge into injectivity of that rectangular column map, and `GeneralizedQRFactorization.A_Q2_mgs_norm_ne_zero_of_nullIntersectionTrivial` plus `GeneralizedQRFactorization.A_Q2_mgs_norm_ne_zero_of_stackedFullColumnRank` feed that injectivity into the exact-MGS all-stage nonbreakdown theorem.  This closes the oracle-recommended nonbreakdown input for the smaller `A Q2` block, but still does not build the full associated (20.28) shape, construct the final `U`, or prove computed GQR stability.  Validation: `lake env lean LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/LeastSquares/LSE.lean`, `git diff --check -- LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/LeastSquares/LSE.lean`, merge with latest `origin/main`, and push to branch and `main` passed.

Additional Theorem 20.9 construction-level AQ2 increment: `gqrAQ2_kernel_trivial_of_constraint_block_nullIntersection`, `gqrAQ2_rectMatMulVec_injective_of_constraint_block_nullIntersection`, and `gqrAQ2_mgs_norm_ne_zero_of_constraint_block_nullIntersection` move the same `A Q2` bridge off the supplied-`GeneralizedQRFactorization` surface.  They need only an orthogonal `Q`, the constructed constraint identity `B Q = [S 0]`, and the local null-intersection assumption.  `exists_gqr_constraint_block_and_A_Q2_mgs_of_fullRowRank_stackedFullColumnRank` combines the exact-MGS construction of the `B^T` side from full row rank of `B` with stacked full column rank to produce a constructed constraint block and all exact-MGS nonbreakdown hypotheses for the smaller `A Q2` block. Additional Theorem 20.9 AQ2 QR-witness increment: `exists_gqr_constraint_block_and_A_Q2_mgs_qr_of_fullRowRank_stackedFullColumnRank` packages the constructed `A Q2` exact-MGS nonbreakdown into explicit QR data: a constructed constraint-side orthogonal `Q`, lower-triangular `S`, an orthonormal-column `Q2`, an upper-triangular `R2`, and the exact factorization `A Q2 = Q2 R2`. Additional Theorem 20.9 source-rank LSE uniqueness increment: `exists_unique_lse_minimizer_of_fullRowRank_stackedFullColumnRank` and `exists_lse_minimizer_of_fullRowRank_stackedFullColumnRank` consume the constructed exact GQR method package and expose the source-facing consequence directly, with no supplied GQR factors or method coordinates in the theorem statement. Additional (20.20)-(20.21) WKS matrix-only RHS-decay dependency: `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorEtaF_minimizer_deltab_norm_le_matrixOnlyEtaF_div_theta` proves that any exact finite-positive `theta` minimizer has `||Delta b||_2` bounded by the matrix-only infimum divided by `theta`; `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorEtaF_exists_feasible_cost_eq_deltab_norm_le_matrixOnlyEtaF_div_theta` packages this with the positive-`theta` minimum-attainment theorem.  `lsNormwiseBackwardErrorEtaF_eventually_exists_feasible_cost_eq_deltab_norm_lt_atTop` upgrades the bound to the filter form: for every positive tolerance, sufficiently large finite weights admit exact minimizers with `||Delta b||_2` below that tolerance.  This proves the quantitative vanishing side needed by the compactness route to the `theta = infinity` reverse inequality, but it does not yet extract a convergent matrix-perturbation limit or prove equality with the matrix-only infimum. Additional Theorem 20.8 perturbed-feasibility decomposition increment: `theorem20_8Projection_apply_of_constraint`, `theorem20_8_perturbed_feasible_difference_constraint`, `theorem20_8Projection_apply_perturbed_feasible_difference`, `theorem20_8_perturbed_feasible_difference_decomp`, and `theorem20_8_perturbed_feasible_point_decomp` prove the exact constraint algebra needed before the perturbation-transfer proof.  If `x` is feasible for `(B,d)` and `y` is feasible for `(B+DeltaB,d+Deltad)`, then the source-side difference satisfies `B*(y-x)=Deltad-DeltaB*y`; applying `P = I - B^+B` decomposes `y-x` into a projected nullspace component plus the `B^+` correction, and the same identity is lifted to a full decomposition of `y`.  This is still exact algebra only: it does not prove the Eldén--Cox--Higham first-order norm bound, discharge pseudoinverse/projector assumptions from (20.24), or formalize the `O(eps^2)` term. Additional Theorem 20.8 Gram determinant obstruction increment: `theorem20_8_gram_AP_rectGram_det_zero_counterexample` proves that the source (20.24)-style rank assumptions do not imply the concrete determinant condition `det(rectGram(AP)) != 0` needed by the Gram pseudoinverse shortcut.  The counterexample uses `A = I_3`, `B` selecting the first coordinate, and the obvious right inverse `Bplus`; then `B*Bplus = I`, `B` is full row rank, `[A; B]` is full column rank, but `AP = A*(I-Bplus*B)` has singular Gram matrix.  This is a route audit, not a closure theorem: it rules out source-deriving the determinant-facing `(AP)^+` branch and leaves the rank-tolerant Moore--Penrose construction or instantiation plus the residual-gap estimate as the viable Theorem 20.8 tasks. The proof combines the previous nonendpoint compressed-eigenvector transfer, the selected-top `lambda = 1` endpoint transfer, and the nonnegativity of the PSD compression spectra to cover the remaining zero endpoint.  This closes the spectral comparison at the selected-top level; it still needs to be packaged through the existing top-eigenvalue/operator-norm wrappers to prove the two `D^2` compression operator norms equal, then the Stewart--Sun cross-projection norm equality, the final Lemma 20.12 `min` surface, and full Theorem 20.1.

Additional Lemma 20.12 compression and cross-projection norm-equality increment: `wedinLemma20_12_complexMatrixOp2_projectionDiff_sq_compression_eq_swapped_of_range_finrank_eq` packages the selected-top equality through the PSD top-eigenvalue/operator-norm wrappers to prove equality of the exact complexified Euclidean operator norms of `P(P-Q)^2P` and `Q(P-Q)^2Q` when the projection ranges have equal finrank.  `wedinLemma20_12_complexMatrixOp2_crossProjection_eq_of_range_finrank_eq` then feeds that into the existing square-root bridge and proves the Stewart--Sun cross-projection norm equality `||P(I-Q)||₂ = ||Q(I-P)||₂` in the `complexMatrixOp2` API, for nonempty ambient dimension.  This closes the abstract principal-angle norm equality needed by Lemma 20.12; the next source-facing step is to instantiate it for `A*Aplus` and `B*Bplus` and remove the conditional equality hypothesis from the `delta * min` projector bound.

Additional Lemma 20.12 source `min` surface increment: `wedinLemma20_12_complexMatrixOp2_rangeProjection_crossProjection_eq_of_left_inverses` instantiates the abstract Stewart--Sun cross-projection equality for the source range projections `A*Aplus` and `B*Bplus`, deriving equal projection-range finrank from the left inverses.  `wedinLemma20_12_rectOpNorm2Le_rangeProjection_mul_projectionComplement_swapped_min` feeds that equality into the existing projector-bound packaging and proves the source-facing `rectOpNorm2Le` surface `||P_B(I-P_A)||₂ <= ||A-B||₂ * min(||Aplus||₂, ||Bplus||₂)` for nonempty ambient dimension.  This removes the last conditional principal-angle equality hypothesis from the Lemma 20.12 bound; the next Theorem 20.1 step is to replace the older conditional/one-sided Wedin perturbation handoff with this unconditional `min` surface.

Additional Theorem 20.1 Lemma 20.12 handoff increment: `wedinTheorem20_1_solutionRelativeRHS_le_of_residual_definitions_min_surface_column_orthogonal` and `wedinTheorem20_1_residualRelativeRHS_le_of_residual_definitions_min_surface_geometry_column_orthogonal` feed the proved Lemma 20.12 source `min` projector bound into the printed Wedin solution and residual RHS wrappers.  The new surfaces derive the needed `Bplus` operator bound from Lemma 20.11, weaken `delta * min(Aplus_norm,Bplus_norm)` to `delta * Aplus_norm` by `rectOpNorm2Le_mono`, and remove both the old raw projector-bound hypothesis and the old cross-projection equality hypothesis. Additional Theorem 20.7 printed-rowwise domination route-blocker: `theorem20_7_compactActiveStepFactor_not_le_rowwise_step_growth_factor_nine` proves that the current compact active-step factor cannot be bounded by `rowwise_step_growth_factor` in general. ## Next Row

- The latest weighted active-ratio reduction means the row-policy path only needs the source unweighted active-ratio maximum plus `|b_i| <= phi * initialRowMax_i` and row nonzero witnesses to recover the weighted active-ratio maximum.  The next Theorem 20.7 row-policy target should therefore be a source-side discharge of the unweighted finite active-ratio `<= sqrt(m)` and RHS domination hypotheses, not another separate weighted-ratio wrapper.
- The same reduction is now wired into the concrete stored-Householder active-tail wrapper.  Downstream stored-QR callers should prefer the `_activeRatioMax_le_of_abs_b_le_nat` surface when their RHS is dominated by the scaled source row maximum, and should reserve the older two-max surface for cases where the weighted finite ratio is proved independently.
- The determinant/lower-prefix nonbreakdown route now has the same single-active-ratio surface, so the next wrapper layer to update is whichever row-policy route is selected next: upper-triangular/local-diagonal convenience wrappers, row-permuted compact-budget wrappers, or the H19 signed-stage completion-preservation route.
